abilities.desc: Powers up Bug-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Hustle
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat but lowers its accuracy.

abilities.name: Truant
abilities.desc: Each time the Pokémon uses a move, it spends the next turn loafing around.

zukanDescs.desc: When it encounters something, its first urge is usually to bite it. If it likes what it tastes, it will commit the associated scent to memory.
Because it can't see, this Pokémon is constantly biting at everything it touches, trying to keep track of its surroundings.
It can't see, so its first approach to examining things is to bite them. You will be covered in wounds until a Deino warms up to you.
It nests deep inside a cave. Food there is scarce, so Deino will sink its teeth into anything that moves and attempt to eat it.

abilities.name: Hustle
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat but lowers its accuracy.

zukanDescs.desc: While hunting for prey, Zweilous wanders its territory, its two heads often bickering over which way to go.
Their two heads will fight each other over a single piece of food. Zweilous are covered in scars even without battling others.
The two heads do not get along at all. If you don't give each head the same amount of attention, they'll begin fighting out of jealousy.
The two heads have different likes and dislikes. Because the heads fight with each other, Zweilous gets stronger without needing to rely on others.

abilities.name: Hustle
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat but lowers its accuracy.

zukanDescs.desc: There are a slew of stories about villages that were destroyed by Hydreigon. It bites anything that moves.
The three heads take turns sinking their teeth into the opponent. Their attacks won't slow until their target goes down.
Only the central head has a brain. It is very intelligent, but it thinks only of destruction.
It's said that Hydreigon grew ferocious because people in times long past loathed it, considering it to be evil incarnate and attacking it relentlessly.

abilities.name: Levitate
abilities.desc: By floating in the air, the Pokémon receives full immunity to all Ground-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: The people of ancient times believed that Larvesta fell from the sun.
Larvesta's body is warm all over. It spouts fire from the tips of its horns to intimidate predators and scare prey.
This Pokémon was called the Larva That Stole the Sun. The fire Larvesta spouts from its horns can cut right through a sheet of iron.
In ancient times, Larvesta was worshiped as the emissary of the sun. However, it was also viewed as a burden since it often caused forest fires.

abilities.name: Flame Body
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

abilities.name: Swarm
abilities.desc: Powers up Bug-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

zukanDescs.desc: Volcarona scatters burning scales. Some say it does this to start fires. Others say it's trying to rescue those that suffer in the cold.
This Pokémon emerges from a cocoon formed of raging flames. Ancient murals depict Volcarona as a deity of fire.
Its burning body causes it to be unpopular in hot parts of the world, but in cold ones, Volcarona is revered as an embodiment of the sun.
This Pokémon scatters burning scales. Most of the danger of these scales is not in their heat—it's in the way they rob the surrounding air of oxygen.

abilities.name: Flame Body
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

abilities.name: Swarm
abilities.desc: Powers up Bug-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon appears in a legend alongside Terrakion and Virizion, fighting against humans in defense of the Unova region's Pokémon.
From the moment it's born, this Pokémon radiates the air of a leader. Its presence will calm even vicious foes.
It has a body and heart of steel. One glare from Cobalion can bring even the most ferocious of Pokémon to heel.

abilities.name: Justified
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a Dark-type attack, its Attack stat is boosted by its sense of justice.

zukanDescs.desc: It has phenomenal power. It will mercilessly crush anyone or anything that bullies small Pokémon.
In Unovan legend, Terrakion battled against humans in an effort to protect other Pokémon.
It charges with enough force to break through a giant castle wall in one blow. This Pokémon is spoken of in legends.

abilities.name: Justified
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a Dark-type attack, its Attack stat is boosted by its sense of justice.

zukanDescs.desc: A legend tells of this Pokémon working together with Cobalion and Terrakion to protect the Pokémon of the Unova region.
It darts around opponents with a flurry of quick movements, slicing them up with its horns.
It confounds opponents with its quick movement, darting around them like a whirlwind as it carves them up mercilessly with its sharp horns.

abilities.name: Justified
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a Dark-type attack, its Attack stat is boosted by its sense of justice.

zukanDescs.desc: This storm-stirring Pokémon is said to cause the seasons to turn by whipping up the air. I suspect its humanlike form to be a false one.

abilities.name: Prankster
abilities.desc: Gives priority to the Pokémon's status moves.

abilities.name: Defiant
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon has any stat lowered by an opposing Pokémon, its Attack stat will be boosted sharply.

zukanDescs.desc: An example of what is known as a "form change," though I suspect this strange avian guise to be Tornadus's true form. Tornadus has been sighted crossing the ocean while in this form.

abilities.name: Regenerator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon has a little of its HP restored when withdrawn from battle.

zukanDescs.desc: They say this wielder of electricity has waged war with its nemesis, Tornadus, since time immemorial. The lightning bolts it hurls pierce the very earth and enrich the soil.

abilities.name: Prankster
abilities.desc: Gives priority to the Pokémon's status moves.

abilities.name: Defiant
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon has any stat lowered by an opposing Pokémon, its Attack stat will be boosted sharply.

zukanDescs.desc: Quick as a flash, it materializes out of nowhere. It pulverizes foes into nothingness with showers of devastatingly powerful lightning bolts launched from the string of orbs on its tail.

abilities.name: Volt Absorb
abilities.desc: If hit by an Electric-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

zukanDescs.desc: Flames spew from its tail as it flies through the sky like a jet airplane. It's said that this Pokémon will scorch the world.
According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down.

abilities.name: Turboblaze
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

zukanDescs.desc: When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings.
Mythology tells us that if people lose the righteousness in their hearts, their kingdoms will be razed by Zekrom's lightning.

abilities.name: Teravolt
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

zukanDescs.desc: When the incarnations of wind and of lightning clash, Landorus arrives to quell the conflict. After the tempests and thunderbolts abate, the land is sure to be blessed with bountiful harvests.

abilities.name: Sand Force
abilities.desc: Boosts the power of Rock-, Ground-, and Steel-type moves in a sandstorm.

abilities.name: Sheer Force
abilities.desc: Removes any additional effects from the Pokémon's moves, but increases the moves' power.

zukanDescs.desc: Landorus soars through the sky in this form, bestowing plentiful harvests upon the land and earning the people's reverence. It can traverse the whole of Hisui in a mere couple of hours.

abilities.name: Intimidate
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it intimidates opposing Pokémon and makes them cower, lowering their Attack stats.

zukanDescs.desc: Dwelling within it is a power even greater than that of Reshiram or Zekrom, but the extreme cold keeps that power bound.
It appears that this Pokémon uses its powers over ice to freeze its own body in order to stabilize its cellular structure.

abilities.name: Pressure
abilities.desc: Puts other Pokémon under pressure, causing them to expend more PP to use their moves.

zukanDescs.desc: The sameness of Reshiram's and Kyurem's genes allowed Kyurem to absorb Reshiram. Kyurem can now use the power of both fire and ice.
It has foreseen that a world of truth will arrive for people and Pokémon. It strives to protect that future.

abilities.name: Turboblaze
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

zukanDescs.desc: It's said that this Pokémon battles in order to protect the ideal world that will exist in the future for people and Pokémon.
The sameness of Zekrom's and Kyurem's genes allowed Kyurem to absorb Zekrom. Kyurem can now use the power of both electricity and ice.

abilities.name: Teravolt
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

zukanDescs.desc: Cobalion, Terrakion, and Virizion taught this Pokémon how to fight. It dashes across the world, seeking more opportunities to further its training.
They say that Keldeo must survive harsh battles and fully develop the horn on its forehead before this Pokémon's true power will awaken.
By shooting water from its hooves, Keldeo can travel across the water's surface as if skating. It's skilled at using kicking moves in battle.

abilities.name: Justified
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a Dark-type attack, its Attack stat is boosted by its sense of justice.

zukanDescs.desc: Keldeo has strengthened its resolve for battle, filling its body with power and changing its form.
The power that lay hidden in its body now covers its horn, turning it into a sword that can slice through anything.
Keldeo's magnificent horn is proof of its resolve. Its allies provided guidance, and their hopes became Keldeo's tricolored plume of feathers.

abilities.name: Justified
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by a Dark-type attack, its Attack stat is boosted by its sense of justice.

zukanDescs.desc: Sung using a special vocalization technique, Meloetta's songs can manipulate the emotions of those who hear them.

abilities.name: Serene Grace
abilities.desc: Raises the likelihood of additional effects occurring when the Pokémon uses its moves.

zukanDescs.desc: Now that it has reclaimed a long-forgotten song, Meloetta captivates everyone with its enchanting dance steps.

abilities.name: Serene Grace
abilities.desc: Raises the likelihood of additional effects occurring when the Pokémon uses its moves.

zukanDescs.desc: This bug Pokémon of the Paleozoic era was revived by a certain organization, which also upgraded the cannon on Genesect's back.

abilities.name: Download
abilities.desc: The Pokémon compares an opposing Pokémon's Defense and Sp. Def stats before raising its own Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever will be more effective.

zukanDescs.desc: Genesect assumes its flight configuration to soar through the sky. Its current form is equipped with a cassette loaded with water energy.

abilities.name: Download
abilities.desc: The Pokémon compares an opposing Pokémon's Defense and Sp. Def stats before raising its own Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever will be more effective.

zukanDescs.desc: Genesect fires off projectile energy blasts. Its current form is equipped with a cassette loaded with electric energy.

abilities.name: Download
abilities.desc: The Pokémon compares an opposing Pokémon's Defense and Sp. Def stats before raising its own Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever will be more effective.

zukanDescs.desc: Genesect possesses sharp steel claws. Its current form is equipped with a cassette loaded with fire energy.

abilities.name: Download
abilities.desc: The Pokémon compares an opposing Pokémon's Defense and Sp. Def stats before raising its own Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever will be more effective.

zukanDescs.desc: Genesect is devoid of mercy. Its current form is equipped with a cassette loaded with ice energy.

abilities.name: Download
abilities.desc: The Pokémon compares an opposing Pokémon's Defense and Sp. Def stats before raising its own Attack or Sp. Atk stat—whichever will be more effective.

zukanDescs.desc: When Chespin tenses up just before landing a headbutt, the spikes on its head sharpen to points, piercing the enemy's body on contact.
Eating lots of berries makes the bark shell that covers Chespin's head and body grow sturdier.
The quills on its head are usually soft. When it flexes them, the points become so hard and sharp that they can pierce rock.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Bulletproof
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from ball and bomb moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It's protected by a sturdy shell. The shell is also very heavy, which naturally strengthens Quilladin's lower body.
It can send a trailer flying with a tackle. It's numb to pain, so even violent collisions don't faze it.
They strengthen their lower bodies by running into one another. They are very kind and won't start fights.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Bulletproof
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from ball and bomb moves.

zukanDescs.desc: When its allies are in trouble, Chesnaught creates a shield with its arms and uses its own body to block attacks.
Its gentle disposition would have it avoid conflict, but it gives opponents a thrashing with its thorned fists when the situation calls for it.
Its tackle is forceful enough to flip a 50-ton tank. It shields its allies from danger with its own body.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Bulletproof
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from ball and bomb moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It has fortified armor and a will to defend at all costs. Both are absurdly strong.
This Pokémon gets worked up easily. Its body can also overheat, so it uses its ears to dissipate the heat and calm down.
Twigs make for Fennekin's snacks. When it nibbles on twigs, it finds the courage to face strong foes.
Eating a twig fills it with energy, and its roomy ears give vent to air hotter than 390 degrees Fahrenheit.

abilities.name: Blaze
abilities.desc: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Magician
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from any target it hits with a move.

zukanDescs.desc: It ignites the twig from its tail and waves the twig around to signal its allies. To indicate danger, it draws circles in the air with the twig's flame.
Braixen's branch ignites as it's pulled from the Pokémon's tail. Braixen then uses the flame to spark powerful attacks.
It keeps a twig in its tail. Using friction from its tail fur, it sets the twig on fire and launches into battle.

abilities.name: Blaze
abilities.desc: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Magician
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from any target it hits with a move.

zukanDescs.desc: It leaves burn marks in the ground with the flame at the tip of its wand. Long ago, people used the shapes of these burn marks for divination.
It controls flames telekinetically, trapping its foes in a fiery vortex surpassing 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit and burning them to a crisp.
Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame.

abilities.name: Blaze
abilities.desc: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Magician
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from any target it hits with a move.

zukanDescs.desc: It wields flaming branches to dazzle its opponents before incinerating them with a huge fireball.
Its happy-go-lucky look is an act intended to fool its enemies. It will suddenly drop the facade and overwhelm its foes with its blinding speed.
It utilizes its extraordinary jumping abilities to flummox its foes, throwing springy bubbles at them from above their heads.
It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

abilities.name: Torrent
abilities.desc: Powers up Water-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Protean
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once each time the Pokémon enters battle.

zukanDescs.desc: It excels at devising battle strategies that make use of the terrain, and it never misses its mark when throwing bubble-covered pebbles.
The tips of its fingers and toes stick firmly to vertical surfaces. Frogadier can scale skyscrapers with ease.
Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute's time.

abilities.name: Torrent
abilities.desc: Powers up Water-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Protean
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once each time the Pokémon enters battle.

zukanDescs.desc: The movement of air against the long tongue Greninja keeps wrapped around its neck allows it to sense enemies it can't see.
It makes shuriken out of mucus excreted from its thighs. These shuriken can cut cleanly through steel cables.
It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

abilities.name: Torrent
abilities.desc: Powers up Water-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Protean
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once each time the Pokémon enters battle.

zukanDescs.desc: It creates throwing stars out of compressed water. When it spins them and throws them at high speed, these stars can split metal in two.

abilities.name: Battle Bond
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon knocks out a target, its bond with its Trainer is strengthened, and its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats are boosted.

abilities.name: Battle Bond
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon knocks out a target, its bond with its Trainer is strengthened, and its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats are boosted.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon spins a giant shuriken at high speed to make it float, then clings to it upside down to catch opponents unawares.
It excels at digging holes. Using its ears, it can dig a nest 33 feet deep in one night.
It's very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight's flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.
They use their large ears to dig burrows. They will dig the whole night through without rest.

abilities.name: Pickup
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

abilities.name: Cheek Pouch
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's HP is restored when it eats any Berry, in addition to the Berry's usual effect.

abilities.name: Huge Power
abilities.desc: Doubles the Pokémon's Attack stat.

zukanDescs.desc: With power equal to an excavator, it can dig through dense bedrock. It's a huge help during tunnel construction.
The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.
With their powerful ears, they can heft boulders of a ton or more with ease. They can be a big help at construction sites.

abilities.name: Pickup
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

abilities.name: Cheek Pouch
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's HP is restored when it eats any Berry, in addition to the Berry's usual effect.

abilities.name: Huge Power
abilities.desc: Doubles the Pokémon's Attack stat.

zukanDescs.desc: Its melodious cries are actually warnings. Fletchling will mercilessly peck at anything that enters its territory.
When this Pokémon gets excited, its body temperature increases sharply. If you touch a Fletchling with bare hands, you might get burned.
This Pokémon is normally calm, but once it enters battle, its hormonal balance changes and it becomes aggressive.
Fletchling can be found in both rural and urban areas. They once had a huge territorial dispute with Squawkabilly. Fights broke out all over town.
Despite the beauty of its lilting voice, it's merciless to intruders that enter its territory.

abilities.name: Big Pecks
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon from having its Defense stat lowered.

abilities.name: Gale Wings
abilities.desc: Gives priority to the Pokémon's Flying-type moves while its HP is full.

zukanDescs.desc: Fletchinder launches embers into the den of its prey. When the prey comes leaping out, Fletchinder's sharp talons finish it off.
Fletchinder are exceedingly territorial and aggressive. These Pokémon fight among themselves over feeding grounds.
Fletchinder scatters embers in tall grass where bug Pokémon might be hiding and then catches them as they come leaping out.
Its territory has a radius of just over a mile. If any bird Pokémon should enter, it will show them no mercy.
The hotter the flame sac in its belly, the faster it can fly, but it takes some time to get the fire going.

abilities.name: Flame Body
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

abilities.name: Gale Wings
abilities.desc: Gives priority to the Pokémon's Flying-type moves while its HP is full.

zukanDescs.desc: Talonflame mainly preys upon other bird Pokémon. To intimidate opponents, it sends embers spewing from gaps between its feathers.
Talonflame dives toward prey at speeds of up to 310 mph and assaults them with powerful kicks, giving the prey no chance to escape.
It has top-notch flying capabilities. It flies around easily, even while carrying prey that weighs more than 220 lbs.
When it gets excited, it vents sparks from the gaps between its feathers. These unburnable feathers are used as a fireproof material.
In the fever of an exciting battle, it showers embers from the gaps between its feathers as it flies.

abilities.name: Flame Body
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

abilities.name: Gale Wings
abilities.desc: Gives priority to the Pokémon's Flying-type moves while its HP is full.

zukanDescs.desc: Any poison this Pokémon takes in is converted into a black powder and secreted. That's why Scatterbug can eat poisonous leaves and roots.
This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder to repel enemies. It will eat different plants depending on where it lives.
The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

abilities.name: Shield Dust
abilities.desc: Protective dust shields the Pokémon from the additional effects of moves.

abilities.name: Compound Eyes
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's compound eyes boost its accuracy.

abilities.name: Friend Guard
abilities.desc: Reduces damage dealt to allies.

zukanDescs.desc: If Rufflet attacks this Pokémon by pecking at it, it will retaliate with its sharp fur and poisonous black powder.
Spewpa doesn't live in a fixed location. It roams where it pleases across the fields and mountains, building up the energy it needs to evolve.
It lives hidden within thicket shadows. When predators attack, it bristles the fur covering its body in an effort to threaten them.

zukanDescs.desc: When Litleo are young, female Pyroar will teach them how to hunt. Once the Litleo mature, they will leave the pride and set out on their own.
The more a Litleo trains its body and spirit by battling mighty enemies, the hotter its mane will grow.
They set off on their own from their pride and live by themselves to become stronger. These hot- blooded Pokémon are quick to fight.

abilities.name: Rivalry
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's competitive spirit makes it deal more damage to Pokémon of the same gender, but less damage to Pokémon of the opposite gender.

abilities.name: Unnerve
abilities.desc: Unnerves opposing Pokémon and makes them unable to eat Berries.

abilities.name: Moxie
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon knocks out a target, it shows moxie, which boosts its Attack stat.

zukanDescs.desc: The females of a pride work together to bring down prey. It's thanks to them that their pride doesn't starve.
The mane of a male Pyroar heats up to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit during battle. Merely approaching it will cause severe burns.
The male with the largest mane of fire is the leader of the pride.

This Pokémon can draw forth the power hidden within blooming wild flowers. It is particularly fond of red flowers.
This Flabébé rides a red flower. Immediately after birth, this Pokémon begins flying around in search of a flower it likes.

When evening falls, it searches out a place blooming with flowers of the same white color as itself, and then it goes to sleep.

This Pokémon creates an impressive flower garden in its territory. It draws forth the power of the red flowers around its neck.
They say that flower gardens created by Florges are constantly showered with a power that can heal both body and spirit.

zukanDescs.desc: Until recently, people living in the mountains would ride on the backs of these Pokémon to traverse the mountain paths.
As long as it has sunlight and water, Skiddo can make energy with the leaves on its body, allowing it to live on rocky mountains barren of food.
If it has sunshine and water, it doesn't need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Grass-type moves. Instead, its Attack stat is boosted.

abilities.name: Grass Pelt
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Defense stat on Grassy Terrain.

zukanDescs.desc: It can sense the feelings of others by touching them with its horns. This species has assisted people with their work since 5,000 years ago.
These Pokémon live in herds in mountainous places. The victor in their contests of clashing horns will become the leader of the herd.
It can tell how its Trainer is feeling by subtle shifts in their grip on its horns. This empathic sense lets human and Pokémon run as if one being.

zukanDescs.desc: It chooses a Pangoro as its master and then imitates its master's actions. This is how it learns to battle and hunt for prey.
Wanting to make sure it's taken seriously, Pancham's always giving others a glare. But if it's not focusing, it ends up smiling.
It does its level best to glare and pull a scary face, but it can't help grinning if anyone pats its head.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon is quick to anger, and it has no problem using its prodigious strength to get its way. It lives for duels against Obstagoon.
Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.
Although it has a violent temperament, it won't put up with those who bully the weak. It senses foes' movements with its leaf.

zukanDescs.desc: Though Espurr's expression never changes, behind that blank stare is an intense struggle to contain its devastating psychic power.
There's enough psychic power in Espurr to send a wrestler flying, but because this power can't be controlled, Espurr finds it troublesome.
It keeps its ears covered to prevent the intense psychic power in its body from escaping, but it falls unconscious if this power builds too much.
Behind an Espurr's expressionless face is a frantic struggle to contain psychic power.
It keeps its intense psychic power from leaking out by using its ears to cover the organs emitting that power.

abilities.name: Keen Eye
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's keen eyes prevent its accuracy from being lowered.

abilities.name: Infiltrator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unaffected by the target's barriers, substitutes, and the like.

abilities.name: Own Tempo
abilities.desc: The Pokémon sticks to its own tempo, preventing it from becoming confused or being affected by Intimidate.

zukanDescs.desc: Revealing the eyelike patterns on the insides of its ears will unleash its psychic powers. It normally keeps the patterns hidden, however.
The defensive instinct of the males is strong. It's when they're protecting themselves or their partners that they unleash their full power.
Though it has enough psychic power to blast away any opponent, Meowstic prefers to support its allies rather than attack.
If Meowstic continuously emits its powerful psychic energy, it will damage its own body.
When in danger, it raises its ears and releases enough psychic power to grind a 10-ton truck into dust.

abilities.name: Keen Eye
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's keen eyes prevent its accuracy from being lowered.

abilities.name: Infiltrator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unaffected by the target's barriers, substitutes, and the like.

abilities.name: Prankster
abilities.desc: Gives priority to the Pokémon's status moves.

zukanDescs.desc: Females are a bit more selfish and aggressive than males. If they don't get what they want, they will torment you with their psychic abilities.
If it doesn't hold back when it unleashes its psychic power, it can tear apart a tanker. Its unfriendliness is part of its charm.
Female Meowstic are more aggressive than males. Their expression remains blank even while they're wielding immense psychic power.
When they sense danger, they exert their psychic power at its maximum output. They have no regard for their opponents.
Females are a bit more selfish and aggressive than males. If they don't get what they want, they will torment you with their psychic abilities.

abilities.name: Keen Eye
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's keen eyes prevent its accuracy from being lowered.

abilities.name: Infiltrator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unaffected by the target's barriers, substitutes, and the like.

abilities.name: Competitive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat sharply when its stats are lowered by an opposing Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: Mega Meowstic can use its psychic power to compress or expand anything. It overwhelms foes by contorting space itself.
Honedge's soul once belonged to a person who was killed a long time ago by the sword that makes up Honedge's body.
The blue eye on the sword's handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people's lives away.
Apparently Honedge was born when a departed spirit began inhabiting an ancient sword. It attaches itself to people and drinks their life force.

abilities.name: No Guard
abilities.desc: The Pokémon employs no-guard tactics to ensure incoming and outgoing attacks always land.

zukanDescs.desc: Honedge evolves into twins. The two blades rub together to emit a metallic sound that unnerves opponents.
The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.
When Honedge evolves, it divides into two swords that cooperate via telepathy to coordinate attacks and slash their enemies to ribbons.

abilities.name: No Guard
abilities.desc: The Pokémon employs no-guard tactics to ensure incoming and outgoing attacks always land.

zukanDescs.desc: In this defensive stance, Aegislash uses its steel body and a force field of spectral power to reduce the damage of any attack.
Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.
Apparently, it can detect the innate qualities of leadership. According to legend, whoever it recognizes is destined to rule.

abilities.name: Stance Change
abilities.desc: The Pokémon changes its form to Blade Forme when it uses an attack move and changes to Shield Forme when it uses King's Shield.

zukanDescs.desc: This stance is dedicated to offense. It can cleave any opponent with the strength and weight of its steel blade.
Once upon a time, a king with an Aegislash reigned over the land. His Pokémon eventually drained him of life, and his kingdom fell with him.
This stance is dedicated to offense. It can cleave any opponent with the strength and weight of its steel blade.

abilities.name: Stance Change
abilities.desc: The Pokémon changes its form to Blade Forme when it uses an attack move and changes to Shield Forme when it uses King's Shield.

zukanDescs.desc: A scent pouch within this Pokémon's body allows it to create various scents. A change in its diet will alter the fragrance it produces.
The scent its body gives off enraptures those who smell it. Noble ladies had no shortage of love for Spritzee.
In the past, rather than using perfume, royal ladies carried a Spritzee that would waft a fragrance they liked.

abilities.name: Healer
abilities.desc: Sometimes cures the status conditions of the Pokémon's allies.

abilities.name: Aroma Veil
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon and its allies from effects that prevent the use of moves.

zukanDescs.desc: The scent that constantly emits from its fur is so powerful that this Pokémon's companions will eventually lose their sense of smell.
The scents Aromatisse can produce range from sweet smells that bolster allies to foul smells that sap an opponent's will to fight.
Its scent is so overpowering that, unless they happen to really enjoy the smell, Trainers will have a hard time walking alongside it.

abilities.name: Healer
abilities.desc: Sometimes cures the status conditions of the Pokémon's allies.

abilities.name: Aroma Veil
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon and its allies from effects that prevent the use of moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It eats its own weight in sugar every day. If it doesn't get enough sugar, it becomes incredibly grumpy.
The sweet smell of cotton candy perfumes Swirlix's fluffy fur. This Pokémon spits out sticky string to tangle up its enemies.
To entangle and immobilize its opponents in battle, it extrudes white threads as sweet and sticky as cotton candy.

abilities.name: Sweet Veil
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from falling asleep.

abilities.name: Unburden
abilities.desc: Boosts the Speed stat if the Pokémon's held item is used or lost.

zukanDescs.desc: By taking in a person's scent, it can sniff out their mental and physical condition. It's hoped that this skill will have many medical applications.
Slurpuff's fur contains a lot of air, making it soft to the touch and lighter than it looks.
It can distinguish the faintest of scents. It puts its sensitive sense of smell to use by helping pastry chefs in their work.

abilities.name: Sweet Veil
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from falling asleep.

abilities.name: Unburden
abilities.desc: Boosts the Speed stat if the Pokémon's held item is used or lost.

zukanDescs.desc: It spins while making its luminescent spots flash. These spots allow it to communicate with others by using different patterns of light.
By exposing foes to the blinking of its luminescent spots, Inkay demoralizes them, and then it seizes the chance to flee.
It protects itself from tough foes by flashing its luminescent spots to discourage them.
Being upside-down improves its blood flow and clears its mind, causing the power of its psychic moves to increase dramatically.
It drains foes' will to fight by flashing light at them. It then takes the opportunity to hide itself away.

abilities.name: Contrary
abilities.desc: Reverses any stat changes affecting the Pokémon so that attempts to boost its stats instead lower them— and attempts to lower its stats will boost them.

abilities.name: Suction Cups
abilities.desc: The Pokémon uses suction cups to stay in one spot. This protects it from moves and items that would force it to switch out.

abilities.name: Infiltrator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unaffected by the target's barriers, substitutes, and the like.

zukanDescs.desc: Gazing at its luminescent spots will quickly induce a hypnotic state, putting the observer under Malamar's control.
It's said that Malamar's hypnotic powers played a role in certain history-changing events.
Its hypnotic powers are dangerously strong. Anyone who falls under Malamar's control loses their memories surrounding the event.
It flashes the luminescent spots on its body to hypnotize its opponents, gaining total control over them.
It wields the most compelling hypnotic powers of any Pokémon, and it forces others to do whatever it wants.

abilities.name: Contrary
abilities.desc: Reverses any stat changes affecting the Pokémon so that attempts to boost its stats instead lower them— and attempts to lower its stats will boost them.

abilities.name: Suction Cups
abilities.desc: The Pokémon uses suction cups to stay in one spot. This protects it from moves and items that would force it to switch out.

abilities.name: Infiltrator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unaffected by the target's barriers, substitutes, and the like.

zukanDescs.desc: It uses its colorful lights to overwrite the personalities and memories of others—and to control them.
After two Binacle find a suitably sized rock, they adhere themselves to it and live together. They cooperate to gather food during high tide.
If the two don't work well together, both their offense and defense fall apart. Without good teamwork, they won't survive.
Two Binacle live together on one rock. When they fight, one of them will move to a different rock.

abilities.name: Tough Claws
abilities.desc: Powers up moves that make direct contact.

abilities.name: Sniper
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon's attack lands a critical hit, the attack is powered up even further.

abilities.name: Pickpocket
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from attackers that make direct contact with it.

zukanDescs.desc: Seven Binacle come together to form one Barbaracle. The Binacle that serves as the head gives orders to those serving as the limbs.
Having an eye on each palm allows it to keep watch in all directions. In a pinch, its limbs start to act on their own to ensure the enemy's defeat.
Barbaracle's legs and hands have minds of their own, and they will move independently. But they usually follow the head's orders.

abilities.name: Tough Claws
abilities.desc: Powers up moves that make direct contact.

abilities.name: Sniper
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon's attack lands a critical hit, the attack is powered up even further.

abilities.name: Pickpocket
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from attackers that make direct contact with it.

zukanDescs.desc: It uses its many arms to toy with its opponents. This keeps the head extremely busy.
It drifts in the ocean, blending in with floating seaweed. When other Pokémon come to feast on the seaweed, Skrelp feasts on them instead.
Skrelp looks like a piece of rotten seaweed, so it can blend in with seaweed drifting on the ocean and avoid being detected by enemies.
Skrelp evades its enemies by hiding amid drifting seaweed. It eats rotten seaweed to create its poison.
This Pokémon is a poor swimmer. If it's caught in a fierce storm, it will sometimes get washed far away and become unable to return to its home.
Camouflaged as rotten kelp, this Pokémon sprays liquid poison to take down prey that approach unawares.

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may poison the attacker.

abilities.name: Poison Touch
abilities.desc: May poison a target when the Pokémon makes contact.

abilities.name: Adaptability
abilities.desc: Powers up moves of the same type as the Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: Dragalge uses a poisonous liquid capable of corroding metal to send tankers that enter its territory to the bottom of the sea.
Dragalge generates dragon energy by sticking the plume on its head out above the ocean's surface and bathing it in sunlight.
Using a liquid poison, Dragalge indiscriminately attacks anything that wanders into its territory. This poison can corrode the undersides of boats.
Since Finizen is the better swimmer, Dragalge will approach it under the cover of seaweed, then immobilize it with a poisonous attack.
Their poison is strong enough to eat through the hull of a tanker, and they spit it indiscriminately at anything that enters their territory.

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may poison the attacker.

abilities.name: Poison Touch
abilities.desc: May poison a target when the Pokémon makes contact.

abilities.name: Adaptability
abilities.desc: Powers up moves of the same type as the Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: It spits a liquid that causes the regenerative power of cells to run wild. The liquid is deadly poison to everything other than itself.
Clauncher's claws can fall off during battle, but they'll regenerate. The meat inside the claws is popular as a delicacy in Galar.
By detonating gas that accumulates in its right claw, this Pokémon launches water like a bullet. This is how Clauncher defeats its enemies.
This Pokémon launches water by detonating gas inside its right claw. It snipes flying Pokémon.
Clauncher's claws will regrow if they fall off. The meat inside the claws is edible, but it has a distinct flavor that doesn't appeal to all tastes.
They knock down flying prey by firing compressed water from their massive claws like shooting a pistol.

abilities.name: Mega Launcher
abilities.desc: Powers up pulse moves.

zukanDescs.desc: After using the feelers on its oversized claw to detect the location of prey, Clawitzer launches a cannonball of water at its target.
Clawitzer's right arm is a cannon that launches projectiles made of seawater. Shots from a Clawitzer's cannon arm can sink a tanker.
Clawitzer can move through the water at a speed of 60 knots by using gas inside its body to expel water from the back of its claw.
The cannonballs of seawater that Clawitzer launches from its claw are powerful enough to punch through tanker hulls.
By expelling water from the nozzle in the back of its claw, it can move at a speed of 60 knots.

abilities.name: Mega Launcher
abilities.desc: Powers up pulse moves.

zukanDescs.desc: When spread, the frills on its head act like solar panels, generating the power behind this Pokémon's electric moves.
The sun powers this Pokémon's electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.
The frills on either side of its head have cells that generate electricity when exposed to sunlight.

abilities.name: Dry Skin
abilities.desc: Restores the Pokémon's HP in rain or when it is hit by Water-type moves. Reduces HP in harsh sunlight, and increases the damage received from Fire-type moves.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

abilities.name: Solar Power
abilities.desc: In harsh sunlight, the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat is boosted, but its HP decreases every turn.

zukanDescs.desc: A now-vanished desert culture treasured these Pokémon. Appropriately, when Heliolisk came to the Galar region, treasure came with them.
One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.
They flare their frills and generate energy. A single Heliolisk can generate sufficient electricity to power a skyscraper.

abilities.name: Dry Skin
abilities.desc: Restores the Pokémon's HP in rain or when it is hit by Water-type moves. Reduces HP in harsh sunlight, and increases the damage received from Fire-type moves.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

abilities.name: Solar Power
abilities.desc: In harsh sunlight, the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat is boosted, but its HP decreases every turn.

zukanDescs.desc: This is an ancient Pokémon, revived in modern times. It has a violent disposition, and it'll tear apart anything it gets between its hefty jaws.
This Pokémon is selfish and likes to be pampered. It can also inflict grievous wounds on its Trainer just by playing around.
This Pokémon was restored from a fossil. If something happens that it doesn't like, it throws a tantrum and runs wild.

abilities.name: Strong Jaw
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's strong jaw boosts the power of its biting moves.

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon cannot be knocked out by a single hit as long as its HP is full. One-hit KO moves will also fail to knock it out.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon is from about 100,000,000 years ago. It has the presence of a king, vicious but magnificent.
A single bite of Tyrantrum's massive jaws will demolish a car. This Pokémon was the king of the ancient world.
This Pokémon behaved like a king 100 million years ago. Nothing could stop it.

abilities.name: Strong Jaw
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's strong jaw boosts the power of its biting moves.

abilities.name: Rock Head
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from recoil damage.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon was successfully restored from a fossil. In the past, it lived with others of its kind in cold lands where there were fewer predators.
Amaura is an ancient Pokémon that has gone extinct. Specimens of this species can sometimes be found frozen in ice.
This ancient Pokémon was restored from a part of its body that had been frozen in ice for 100 million years.

abilities.name: Refrigerate
abilities.desc: Normal-type moves become Ice-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

abilities.name: Snow Warning
abilities.desc: The Pokémon makes it snow when it enters a battle.

zukanDescs.desc: Aurorus was restored from a fossil. It's said that when this Pokémon howls, auroras appear in the night sky.
When gripped by rage, Aurorus will emanate freezing air, covering everything around it in ice.
The diamond-shaped crystals on its body expel air as cold as −240 degrees Fahrenheit, surrounding its enemies and encasing them in ice.

abilities.name: Refrigerate
abilities.desc: Normal-type moves become Ice-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

abilities.name: Snow Warning
abilities.desc: The Pokémon makes it snow when it enters a battle.

zukanDescs.desc: By releasing enmity-erasing waves from its ribbonlike feelers, Sylveon stops any conflict.
There's a Galarian fairy tale that describes a beautiful Sylveon vanquishing a dreadful dragon Pokémon.
It emits a soothing aura from its ribbon-shaped organs. It wraps these appendages around quarrelers to instantly restore calm to the situation.
This Pokémon uses its ribbonlike feelers to send a soothing aura into its opponents, erasing their hostility.
Sylveon cuts an elegant figure as it dances lightly around, feelers fluttering, but its piercing moves aim straight for its opponents' weak spots.
It wraps its ribbonlike feelers around the arm of its beloved Trainer and walks with them.

abilities.name: Cute Charm
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may infatuate attackers that make direct contact with it.

abilities.name: Pixilate
abilities.desc: Normal-type moves become Fairy-type moves. The power of those moves is boosted a little.

zukanDescs.desc: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.
It always strikes a pose before going for its finishing move. Sometimes opponents take advantage of that time to counterattack.
Its elegant finishing moves—performed by nimbly leaping around using its wings—are polished in the forest where it was born and raised.
Hawlucha live quietly without flocking together. They fight constantly with their natural enemies: noisy groups of Primeape.
With its wings, it controls its position in the air. It likes to attack from above, a maneuver that is difficult to defend against.

abilities.name: Limber
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's limber body prevents it from being paralyzed.

abilities.name: Unburden
abilities.desc: Boosts the Speed stat if the Pokémon's held item is used or lost.

abilities.name: Mold Breaker
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

zukanDescs.desc: Mega Evolution has pumped up all its muscles. Hawlucha flexes to show off its strength.
A Dedenne's whiskers pick up electrical waves other Dedenne send out. These Pokémon share locations of food or electricity with one another.
Since Dedenne can't generate much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.
It's small and its electricity-generating organ is not fully developed, so it uses its tail to absorb electricity from people's homes and charge itself.
Dedenne emit electrical waves from the whiskers on their cheeks to communicate with each other. When low on electricity, they curl up and sleep.
Its whiskers serve as antennas. By sending and receiving electrical waves, it can communicate with others over vast distances.

abilities.name: Cheek Pouch
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's HP is restored when it eats any Berry, in addition to the Berry's usual effect.

abilities.name: Pickup
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

abilities.name: Plus
abilities.desc: Boosts the Sp. Atk stat of the Pokémon if an ally with the Plus or Minus Ability is also in battle.

zukanDescs.desc: When beset by attackers, Carbink wipes them all out by firing high-energy beams from the gems embedded in its body.
It's said that somewhere in the world, there's a mineral vein housing a large pack of slumbering Carbink. It's also said that this pack has a queen.
It has slept underground for hundreds of millions of years since its birth. It's occasionally found during the excavation of caves.
Born from the high temperatures and pressures deep underground, it defends itself by firing beams from the jewel part of its body.
Born from the high temperatures and pressures deep underground, it releases energy from the stone in its head.

abilities.name: Clear Body
abilities.desc: Prevents other Pokémon's moves or Abilities from lowering the Pokémon's stats.

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon cannot be knocked out by a single hit as long as its HP is full. One-hit KO moves will also fail to knock it out.

zukanDescs.desc: Because most of its body is water, it will dry up if the weather becomes too arid. It's considered the weakest dragon Pokémon.
Their horns are powerful sensors. As soon as Goomy pick up any sign of enemies, they go into hiding. This is how they've survived.
Goomy hides away in the shade of trees, where it's nice and humid. If the slime coating its body dries out, the Pokémon instantly becomes lethargic.
Most of a Goomy's body is water. A membrane covers the whole Pokémon to prevent it from shriveling up in dry weather.
Goomy's horns are excellent sensory organs that cover all five of Goomy's senses. Goomy picks up on danger from the movement of the air.
Thanks to the slimy membrane covering its body, punches or kicks from its enemies slide right off it.

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Grass-type moves. Instead, its Attack stat is boosted.

abilities.name: Hydration
abilities.desc: Cures the Pokémon's status conditions in rain.

abilities.name: Gooey
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

zukanDescs.desc: Although this Pokémon isn't very strong, its body is coated in a caustic slime that can melt through anything, so predators steer clear of it.
The lump on its back contains its tiny brain. It thinks only of food and escaping its enemies.
The swirly protrusion on its back is filled with all its vital organs, such as its brain and heart.
When Sliggoo senses danger, the mucus coating its entire body becomes more concentrated— it'll dissolve anything.
Its four horns are a high-performance radar system. It uses them to sense sounds and smells, rather than using ears or a nose.

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Grass-type moves. Instead, its Attack stat is boosted.

abilities.name: Hydration
abilities.desc: Cures the Pokémon's status conditions in rain.

abilities.name: Gooey
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

zukanDescs.desc: A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.
The iron in Sliggoo's drinking water turned its shell metallic. The Pokémon doesn't come out of its shell on sunny days.

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Grass-type moves. Instead, its Attack stat is boosted.

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.desc: A hard shell protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

abilities.name: Gooey
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

zukanDescs.desc: Sometimes it misunderstands instructions and appears dazed or bewildered. Many Trainers don't mind, finding this behavior to be adorable.
Its form of offense is forcefully stretching out its horns. The strikes land 100 times harder than any blow from a heavyweight boxer.
It loves the rain. This mellow Pokémon can be seen walking around on the plains and in the mountains on rainy days.
This Pokémon is uncontrollable when enraged. It rampages on and on, lashing its tail with enough power to send a dump truck flying.
This very friendly dragon Pokémon will hug its beloved Trainer, leaving that Trainer covered in sticky slime.

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Grass-type moves. Instead, its Attack stat is boosted.

abilities.name: Hydration
abilities.desc: Cures the Pokémon's status conditions in rain.

abilities.name: Gooey
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

zukanDescs.desc: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.
If anything bad happens to it, Goodra retreats into its shell and bounces around like a rubber ball, knocking down trees and boring into mountains.

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Grass-type moves. Instead, its Attack stat is boosted.

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.desc: A hard shell protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

abilities.name: Gooey
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon is constantly collecting keys. Entrust a Klefki with important keys, and the Pokémon will protect them no matter what.
Klefki sucks in metal ions with the horn topping its head. It seems this Pokémon loves keys so much that its head needed to look like one, too.
Once it absorbs a key's metal ions, it discards the key without a second thought. However, it will hang on to keys it favors for decades.
In the past, noble families entrusted their vault keys to a Klefki. They passed the Klefki down through the generations, taking good care of it.
It never lets go of a key that it likes, so people give it the keys to vaults and safes as a way to prevent crime.

abilities.name: Prankster
abilities.desc: Gives priority to the Pokémon's status moves.

abilities.name: Magician
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from any target it hits with a move.

zukanDescs.desc: After a lost child perished in the forest, their spirit possessed a tree stump, causing the spirit's rebirth as this Pokémon.
With a voice like a human child's, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.
Legend tells that its green leaves can cure any illness. When plucked, however, the leaves will instantly wither away.
This Pokémon came to be when the spirit of a deceased child possessed a tree stump. It now wanders the forest searching for friends.
According to old tales, these Pokémon are stumps possessed by the spirits of children who died while lost in the forest.

abilities.name: Natural Cure
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's status conditions are cured when it switches out.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

abilities.name: Harvest
abilities.desc: May create another Berry after one is used.

zukanDescs.desc: People fear it due to a belief that it devours any who try to cut down trees in its forest, but to the Pokémon it shares its woods with, it's kind.
Small roots that extend from the tips of this Pokémon's feet can tie into the trees of the forest and give Trevenant control over them.
Using its roots, Trevenant connects itself to trees and monitors every corner of its forest. It uses curses to drive intruders away.
Trevenant is very kind to Pokémon living in the forest. It doesn't even care if these Pokémon take up residence in the greenery on its head.
Using its roots as a nervous system, it controls the trees in the forest. It's kind to the Pokémon that reside in its body.

abilities.name: Natural Cure
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's status conditions are cured when it switches out.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

abilities.name: Harvest
abilities.desc: May create another Berry after one is used.

zukanDescs.desc: Spirits that wander this world are placed into Pumpkaboo's body. They're then moved on to the afterlife.
The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.
It was recently discovered that the different varieties of Pumpkaboo vary not only in size but also in the size of the souls they collect.

abilities.name: Pickup
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's insomnia prevents it from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: When taking spirits to the afterlife, small Pumpkaboo prefer the spirits of children to those of adults.
Small Pumpkaboo are said to be the product of areas where few lost souls lingered.
In keeping with the size of its body, this variety of Pumpkaboo takes the small spirits of children to the afterlife.

abilities.name: Pickup
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's insomnia prevents it from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: When taking spirits to the afterlife, large Pumpkaboo prefer the spirits of adults to those of children.
Large Pumpkaboo are said to be the product of areas where many lost souls lingered.
In keeping with the size of its body, this variety of Pumpkaboo takes the large spirits of adults to the afterlife.

abilities.name: Pickup
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's insomnia prevents it from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: Supersized Pumpkaboo are very partial to the spirits of people who were of similarly superior proportions.
Massive Pumpkaboo are said to be the product of areas where a great number of lost souls lingered.
In keeping with the size of its body, this variety of Pumpkaboo takes jumbo spirits to the afterlife.

abilities.name: Pickup
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's insomnia prevents it from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: Eerie cries emanate from its body in the dead of night. The sounds are said to be the wails of spirits who are suffering in the afterlife.
In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.
Singing in eerie voices, they wander town streets on the night of the new moon. Anyone who hears their song is cursed.

abilities.name: Pickup
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's insomnia prevents it from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: Small Gourgeist pretend to be children to fool adults. Anyone who falls for the act gets carried away to the hereafter.
A small-sized Pumpkaboo evolves into a small-sized Gourgeist. Its bodily proportions also get passed on to its descendants.
Small Gourgeist pretend to be children to fool adults. Anyone who falls for the act gets carried away to the hereafter.

abilities.name: Pickup

zukanDescs.desc: They chill the air around them to −150 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing the water in the air into ice that they use as armor.
This Pokémon lives in areas of frigid cold. It secures itself to the back of an Avalugg by freezing its feet in place.
Lives on mountains blanketed in perennial snow. It freezes water vapor in the air to make the ice helmet that it dons for defense.
They live in mountainous regions of frigid cold. On rare occasions, they ride on the back of an Avalugg to cross seas and move to new habitats.
Bergmite protects itself by coating its body in ice formed by frigid air of −148 degrees Fahrenheit. It fights with Frigibax whenever they meet.
It blocks opponents' attacks with the ice that shields its body. If the ice breaks, this Pokémon uses cold air to quickly create new ice.

abilities.name: Own Tempo

zukanDescs.desc: At high latitudes, this Pokémon can be found with clusters of Bergmite on its back as it swims among the icebergs.
As Avalugg moves about during the day, the cracks in its body deepen. The Pokémon's body returns to a pristine state overnight.
As Avalugg walks along with Bergmite on its back, it comes across pods of Cetitan. It lets them pass to avoid conflict.
This Pokémon uses its massive icy body to flatten anything that gets in its way. When it's floating out on the ocean, it looks exactly like drift ice.
Its ice-covered body is as hard as steel. Its enormous frame crushes anything that stands in its way.

zukanDescs.desc: The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.
With its icy jaw, it swiftly plows across snowy fields and mountains, in search of nutritious vegetables people have stored in the snow.

abilities.name: Ice Body

zukanDescs.desc: After nightfall, they emerge from the caves they nest in during the day. Using their ultrasonic waves, they go on the hunt for ripened fruit.
No wavelength of sound is beyond Noibat's ability to produce. The ultrasonic waves it generates can overcome much larger Pokémon.
This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from its large ears to search for fruit to eat. It mistakes Applin for its food.
Noibat can change the frequency of its sound waves at will, and it generates ultrasonic waves of up to 200,000 hertz.
Even a robust wrestler will become dizzy and unable to stand when exposed to its 200,000-hertz ultrasonic waves.

abilities.name: Telepathy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon anticipates and dodges the attacks of its allies.

zukanDescs.desc: Aggressive and cruel, this Pokémon will ruthlessly torment enemies that are helpless in the dark.
Flying through the darkness, it weakens enemies with ultrasonic waves that could crush stone. Its fangs finish the fight.
Ultrasonic waves emitted by a Noivern can pulverize a large boulder. This Pokémon has a cruel disposition.
In the dark, even Hydreigon are terrified of this Pokémon. But in the light, Noivern is the one that avoids battle.
They fly around on moonless nights and attack careless prey. Nothing can beat them in a battle in the dark.

zukanDescs.desc: It's hypothesized that it's monitoring those who destroy the ecosystem from deep in the cave where it lives.

zukanDescs.desc: In response to people's emotions during an unprecedented crisis, Zygarde Mega Evolves and calms the situation with its unmatched power.
Diancie is a spontaneous mutation of Carbink. Its pink, glimmering body is said to be the loveliest sight in the whole world.

abilities.name: Clear Body
abilities.desc: Prevents other Pokémon's moves or Abilities from lowering the Pokémon's stats.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon is also known as the Royal Pink Princess. The dazzling, sparkling diamond on its forehead is a whopping 2,000 carats.

abilities.name: Magic Bounce
abilities.desc: The Pokémon reflects status moves instead of getting hit by them.

zukanDescs.desc: It uses loops to gather things it likes and take them to its secret home. It also teleports through a loop.

abilities.name: Magician
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from any target it hits with a move.

zukanDescs.desc: This is Hoopa's true form, in which it has regained its sealed powers. The hole in its chest is said to connect to another dimension.

abilities.name: Magician
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from any target it hits with a move.

zukanDescs.desc: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

abilities.name: Water Absorb
abilities.desc: If hit by a Water-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

zukanDescs.desc: At a distance, it launches its sharp feathers while flying about. If the enemy gets too close, Rowlet switches tactics and delivers vicious kicks.
During the day, it builds up energy via photosynthesis. At night, it flies silently through the sky, on the prowl for prey.
Flies noiselessly on delicate wings. It has mastered the art of deftly launching dagger-sharp feathers from those same wings.
Silently it glides, streaking toward its targets to get close to them. Before they even notice it, it begins to pelt them with vicious kicks.
It feels relaxed in tight, dark places and has been known to nestle into its Trainer's bag or the front of their jacket.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Long Reach
abilities.desc: The Pokémon uses its moves without making contact with the target.

zukanDescs.desc: It throws one knifelike feather after another at its enemies, and each one precisely strikes a weak point. These feathers are known as blade quills.
It never slacks when it comes to the task of cleaning its feathers. Thorough preening keeps it looking spiffy and its blade quills nice and sharp.
Regularly basks in sunlight to gather power—presumably due to the frigid climate. Nonetheless, the edges of the blade quills set into its wings are keen as ever.
This narcissistic Pokémon is a clean freak. If you don't groom it diligently, it may stop listening to you.
It throws sharp feathers called blade quills at enemies or prey. It seldom misses.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Long Reach
abilities.desc: The Pokémon uses its moves without making contact with the target.

zukanDescs.desc: As if wielding a bow, it launches the arrow quills hidden among the feathers of its wings. Decidueye's shots never miss.
In a tenth of a second, it can nock and fire an arrow quill, piercing an opponent's weak point before they notice what's happening.
It cunningly conceals arrow quills in its wings. It launches these quills with such precision that they can pierce a pebble at over a hundred yards.
It nocks its arrow quills and looses them at opponents. When it needs to hit, it pulls the vines on its hood to narrow its view and focus.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Long Reach
abilities.desc: The Pokémon uses its moves without making contact with the target.

zukanDescs.desc: The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Scrappy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can hit Ghost-type Pokémon with Normal- and Fighting-type moves. It is also unaffected by Intimidate.

zukanDescs.desc: It spends even the smallest amount of downtime grooming its fur with its tongue. Loose fur gathers in its stomach and serves as fuel for fiery moves.
Trying to pet Litten before it trusts you will result in a nasty scratch from its sharp claws. Be careful.
If you try too hard to get close to it, it won't open up to you. Even if you do grow close, giving it too much affection is still a no-no.
While grooming itself, it builds up fur inside its stomach. It sets the fur alight and spews fiery attacks, which change based on how it coughs.

abilities.name: Blaze
abilities.desc: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Intimidate
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it intimidates opposing Pokémon and makes them cower, lowering their Attack stats.

zukanDescs.desc: In the midst of battle, the fire pouch on Torracat's neck rings like a bell and produces stronger flames than usual.
When facing a powerful enemy, Torracat's fighting spirit gets pumped up, and its fire bell blazes hotter.
At its throat, it bears a bell of fire. The bell rings brightly whenever fire spews out.
Torracat will let its Trainer coddle it once they've grown close, but it's a powerful, sharp-clawed Pokémon, so its Trainer gets covered in scratches.

abilities.name: Blaze
abilities.desc: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Intimidate

zukanDescs.desc: It excels at violent, no-holds-barred battles. The temperature of the flames that issue from its navel exceeds 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.
Incineroar's rough and aggressive behavior is its most notable trait, but the way it helps out small Pokémon shows that it has a kind side as well.
When its fighting spirit is set alight, the flames around its waist become especially intense.
After hurling ferocious punches and flinging furious kicks, it finishes opponents off by spewing fire from around its navel.

abilities.name: Blaze

abilities.name: Intimidate
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it intimidates opposing Pokémon and makes them cower, lowering their Attack stats.

zukanDescs.desc: It creates special bouncy balloons to send at its opponents. When the balloons pop, the sheer force of their bursting is what deals damage.
If Popplio want to create big, powerful balloons, they must be persistent. It takes daily practice for these Pokémon to develop their skills.
This Pokémon can control balloons made of water. It practices diligently so it can learn to make big balloons.
The balloons it inflates with its nose grow larger and larger as it practices day by day.

abilities.name: Torrent
abilities.desc: Powers up Water-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Liquid Voice

zukanDescs.desc: As if dancing, it artfully dodges the attacks of its enemies. All the while, it's busy forming a bunch of balloons to overwhelm its foes.
On nights when the sea is calm, Brionne dance with one another to the singing of the Primarina that's leading them.
A skillful dancer, it creates a sequence of water balloons as it dances, then briskly bombards its enemies.
It cares deeply for its companions. When its Trainer is feeling down, it performs a cheery dance to try to help.

abilities.name: Torrent

abilities.name: Liquid Voice

zukanDescs.desc: With its mouth, it makes sonic waves that sound like beautiful singing. It uses the sonic waves to control its water balloons.
For Primarina, every battle's a stage. Its singing and the dancing of its balloons will mesmerize the audience.
To Primarina, every battle is a stage. It takes down its prey with beautiful singing and dancing.
Also known as a songstress, it is a sight to behold on moonlit nights when it sings in front of the colony it leads.

abilities.name: Torrent

Pairs of Toucannon are considered symbols of companionship, as these Pokémon will raise the temperature of their beaks to warm each other.

abilities.name: Keen Eye

zukanDescs.desc: Its stomach fills most of its torso. It wanders the same path every day, searching for fresh food.
It spends its waking hours searching endlessly for food. When Yungoos is hungry, its ferocity gains a certain edge.

abilities.name: Strong Jaw

zukanDescs.desc: Once it finds signs of prey, it will patiently stake out the location, waiting until the sun goes down.
Gumshoos specializes in relentlessly tracking down prey. The simple-minded Skwovet doesn't seem to provide Gumshoos enough challenge.

abilities.name: Stakeout

zukanDescs.desc: Its natural enemies, like Rookidee, may flee rather than risk getting caught in its large mandibles that can snap thick tree branches.
It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.
Grubbin normally live underground, but if they get unearthed by Hoothoot, they may be found gathered around electric Pokémon.
It spits sticky threads and winds them around branches, then swings nimbly from tree to tree in a pendulum-like motion.

abilities.name: Swarm
abilities.desc: Powers up Bug-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

zukanDescs.desc: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.
Its digestive processes convert the leaves it eats into electricity. An electric sac in its belly stores the electricity for later use.
Protected by a sturdy shell, Charjabug eats large quantities of fresh and decomposing leaf litter in order to evolve.
As it digests fallen leaves, it generates and stores electricity, which it can discharge from the tips of its jaws.

abilities.name: Battery
abilities.desc: Powers up ally Pokémon's special moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It builds up electricity in its abdomen, focuses it through its jaws, and then fires the electricity off in concentrated beams.
If it carries a Charjabug to use as a spare battery, a flying Vikavolt can rapidly fire high-powered beams of electricity.
Displaying exceptional mobility, it weaves between trees and shoots down bird Pokémon with its electromagnetic beams.
When carrying a Charjabug, Vikavolt can receive electricity from it and then rapidly fire powerful electromagnetic beams from its large jaws.

abilities.name: Levitate
abilities.desc: By floating in the air, the Pokémon receives full immunity to all Ground-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon punches trees and eats the berries that drop down, training itself and getting food at the same time.
If it loses its pincers, they'll quickly regrow. The pincers are popular ingredients in paella since their shells produce a tasty soup stock.
Its hard pincers are well-suited to both offense and defense. Fights between two Crabrawler are like boxing matches.

abilities.name: Hyper Cutter
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's prized, mighty pincers prevent other Pokémon from lowering its Attack stat.

abilities.name: Iron Fist

abilities.name: Anger Point
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is angered when it takes a critical hit, and that maxes its Attack stat.

zukanDescs.desc: The detached pincers of these Pokémon are delicious. Some Trainers bring Lechonk into the mountains just to search for them.
Though its punches are powerful, this Pokémon's movements are sluggish. It blows icy bubbles from its mouth to immobilize its opponents.
It punches things without pausing to think. There are even records of Crabominable forcing back an avalanche with a flurry of punches.

abilities.name: Hyper Cutter
