zukanDescs.desc: It can pulverize reinforced concrete with a light swing of one of its fists, each of which is covered in a thick layer of ice.
This Pokémon is incredibly popular, possibly because its passionate dancing is a great match with the temperament of Paldean people.
This form of Oricorio has sipped red nectar. It whips up blazing flames as it moves to the steps of its passionate dance.

abilities.name: Dancer
abilities.desc: Whenever a dance move is used in battle, the Pokémon will copy the user to immediately perform that dance move itself.

zukanDescs.desc: This form of Oricorio has sipped yellow nectar. It uses nimble steps to approach opponents, then knocks them out with electric punches.
The energetic, cheerful dance of these Oricorio is popular with children, but the Oricorio themselves are hard to find in Paldea.

abilities.name: Dancer
abilities.desc: Whenever a dance move is used in battle, the Pokémon will copy the user to immediately perform that dance move itself.

zukanDescs.desc: The airy dance of these Oricorio is popular as a means of maintaining good health, but the Oricorio themselves are hard to find in Paldea.
This form of Oricorio has sipped pink nectar. It elevates its mind with the gentle steps of its dance, then unleashes its psychic energy.

abilities.name: Dancer
abilities.desc: Whenever a dance move is used in battle, the Pokémon will copy the user to immediately perform that dance move itself.

zukanDescs.desc: This form of Oricorio has sipped purple nectar. It uses ethereal dance steps to call forth the spirits of the dead.
The elegant, attractive dance of these Oricorio is popular with adults, but the Oricorio themselves are hard to find in Paldea.

abilities.name: Dancer
abilities.desc: Whenever a dance move is used in battle, the Pokémon will copy the user to immediately perform that dance move itself.

zukanDescs.desc: Nectar and pollen are its favorite fare. You can find Cutiefly hovering around Gossifleur, trying to get some of Gossifleur's pollen.
An opponent's aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent's next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.
When it senses the auras of people or Pokémon who are having fun, it will draw close and give them a painful poke with its long proboscis.
Cutiefly sucks up pollen and nectar with its long proboscis. It stores any pollen it doesn't finish eating in its fur.

abilities.name: Honey Gather
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may gather Honey after a battle.

abilities.name: Shield Dust
abilities.desc: Protective dust shields the Pokémon from the additional effects of moves.

abilities.name: Sweet Veil
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: It makes pollen puffs from pollen and nectar. The puffs' effects depend on the type of ingredients and how much of each one is used.
Ribombee absolutely hate getting wet or rained on. In the cloudy Galar region, they are very seldom seen.
Ribombee can sense when people or Pokémon are feeling down, and it will cheer them up with handmade pollen puffs.
Pollen puffs created by skilled Ribombee can relieve fatigue, making the puffs a popular commodity.

abilities.name: Honey Gather
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may gather Honey after a battle.

abilities.name: Shield Dust
abilities.desc: Protective dust shields the Pokémon from the additional effects of moves.

abilities.name: Sweet Veil
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon can bond very strongly with its Trainer, but it also has a habit of biting. Raising a Rockruff for a long time can be challenging.
This Pokémon intimidates opponents by striking the ground with the rocks on its neck. The moment an opponent flinches, Rockruff attacks.
This Pokémon is very friendly when it's young. Its disposition becomes vicious once it matures, but it never forgets the kindness of its master.
This Pokémon travels in a pack with others until it grows up. When its mood turns sour, it starts striking the ground with the rocks on its neck.

abilities.name: Keen Eye
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's keen eyes prevent its accuracy from being lowered.

abilities.name: Vital Spirit
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is full of vitality, and that prevents it from falling asleep.

abilities.desc: The Pokémon's determination boosts its Speed stat every time it flinches.

zukanDescs.desc: This Lycanroc is calm and cautious. The rocks jutting from its mane are razor sharp.
With swift movements, this Pokémon gradually backs its prey into a corner. Lycanroc's fangs are always aimed toward opponents' weak spots.
Lycanroc attacks its prey with its sharp claws and fangs. It loyally obeys the instructions of a Trainer it trusts.
It has a calm and collected demeanor. It swiftly closes in on its prey, then slices them with the rocks in its mane.

abilities.name: Keen Eye

abilities.name: Sand Rush
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in a sandstorm.

abilities.name: Steadfast

zukanDescs.desc: This form of Lycanroc is reckless. It charges headlong at its opponents, attacking without any care about what injuries it might receive.
It's invigorated by powerful opponents—the stronger the better. A full-force headbutt from one of these Lycanroc can shatter giant boulders.
This Pokémon uses its rocky mane to slash any who approach. It will even disobey its Trainer if it dislikes the orders it was given.
This Lycanroc has an extremely vicious temperament. It will happily sustain injuries for the sake of taking down its opponent.

abilities.name: Keen Eye

abilities.name: Vital Spirit
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is full of vitality, and that prevents it from falling asleep.

abilities.name: No Guard
abilities.desc: The Pokémon employs no-guard tactics to ensure incoming and outgoing attacks always land.

zukanDescs.desc: This form of Lycanroc is normally calm and quiet. Once a battle begins, however, this Pokémon displays a ferocious fighting spirit.
These Pokémon have both calm and ferocious qualities. Their temperamental nature makes them a difficult species to raise.
Normally this Pokémon doesn't even bark, but once it enters battle, it will relentlessly drive the opponent into a corner.
These Pokémon have both calm and ferocious qualities. It's said that this form of Lycanroc is the most troublesome to raise.

abilities.name: Tough Claws
abilities.desc: Powers up moves that make direct contact.

zukanDescs.desc: Individually, they're incredibly weak. It's by gathering up into schools that they're able to confront opponents.
When it senses danger, its eyes tear up. The sparkle of its tears signals other Wishiwashi to gather.

abilities.name: Schooling
abilities.desc: When it has a lot of HP, the Pokémon forms a powerful school. It stops schooling when its HP is low.

zukanDescs.desc: On their own, they're very weak. But when Wishiwashi pool their power together in a school, they become a demon of the sea.
When facing tough opponents, they get into formation. But if they get wounded in battle, they'll scatter and become solitary again.

abilities.name: Schooling
abilities.desc: When it has a lot of HP, the Pokémon forms a powerful school. It stops schooling when its HP is low.

zukanDescs.desc: The first symptom of its sting is numbness. The next is an itching sensation so intense that it's impossible to resist the urge to claw at your skin.
Unlike their Alolan counterparts, the Mareanie of the Galar region have not yet figured out that the branches of Corsola are delicious.
This Pokémon wanders the seaside looking for food. It often gets electric shocks from broken Pincurchin spines that it tries to eat.
It has excellent regenerative capabilities. Legs that a Mareanie has lost have a very bitter, astringent flavor—they're not suitable as food.

abilities.name: Merciless
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's attacks become critical hits if the target is poisoned.

abilities.name: Limber
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's limber body prevents it from being paralyzed.

abilities.name: Regenerator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon has a little of its HP restored when withdrawn from battle.

zukanDescs.desc: To survive in the cold waters of Galar, this Pokémon forms a dome with its legs, enclosing its body so it can capture its own body heat.
Within the poison sac in its body is a poison so toxic that Pokémon as large as Wailord will still be suffering three days after it first takes effect.
This Pokémon lives within a dome made by its own legs. Toxapex monitors its surroundings by sensing the flow of the tide through its spikes.
Toxapex gets into fierce battles with Bruxish over areas where warm ocean currents flow, but the odds are always against it.

abilities.name: Merciless
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's attacks become critical hits if the target is poisoned.

abilities.name: Limber
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's limber body prevents it from being paralyzed.

abilities.name: Regenerator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon has a little of its HP restored when withdrawn from battle.

zukanDescs.desc: Loads weighing up to 50 times as much as its own body weight pose no issue for this Pokémon. It's skilled at making use of mud.
It eats dirt to create mud and smears this mud all over its feet, giving them the grip needed to walk on rough terrain without slipping.
This Pokémon covers itself in mud that it has regurgitated. The mud won't dry out even if it's exposed to the sun for a long time.
Though a slow walker, Mudbray is plenty strong. Its pace doesn't change even when it's loaded with 50 times its own body weight.

abilities.name: Own Tempo
abilities.desc: The Pokémon sticks to its own tempo, preventing it from becoming confused or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Stamina
abilities.desc: Boosts the Defense stat when the Pokémon is hit by an attack.

abilities.name: Inner Focus
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's intense focus prevents it from flinching or being affected by Intimidate.

zukanDescs.desc: Mud that hardens around a Mudsdale's legs sets harder than stone. It's so hard that it allows this Pokémon to scrap a truck with a single kick.
Mudsdale has so much stamina that it could carry over 10 tons across the Galar region without rest or sleep.
This Pokémon has been treasured not just for its physical labor but also because it produces high-quality mud used for making pottery.
Its legs are fortified with mud and harder than stone, and they can reduce a large truck to scrap with one kick.

abilities.name: Own Tempo
abilities.desc: The Pokémon sticks to its own tempo, preventing it from becoming confused or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Stamina
abilities.desc: Boosts the Defense stat when the Pokémon is hit by an attack.

abilities.name: Inner Focus
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's intense focus prevents it from flinching or being affected by Intimidate.

zukanDescs.desc: It forms a water bubble at the rear of its body and then covers its head with it. Meeting another Dewpider means comparing water-bubble sizes.
Dewpider normally lives underwater. When it comes onto land in search of food, it takes water with it in the form of a bubble on its head.
Dewpider uses sticky threads to create the water bubble that covers its head, so the bubble very rarely bursts.
It supports the water bubble that envelops its head with three of its legs. Sometimes it secretly swaps which legs are holding up the bubble.

abilities.name: Water Bubble
abilities.desc: Lowers the power of Fire-type moves that hit the Pokémon and prevents it from being burned.

abilities.name: Water Absorb
abilities.desc: If hit by a Water-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

zukanDescs.desc: It launches water bubbles with its legs, drowning prey within the bubbles. This Pokémon can then take its time to savor its meal.
It acts as a caretaker for Dewpider, putting them inside its bubble and letting them eat any leftover food.
A caring Pokémon, Araquanid carries Dewpider around inside its water bubble to protect them as it walks. It protects its food in its bubble too.
It launches sticky water bubbles. Foes who lose consciousness while trapped inside these bubbles will get torn apart by Araquanid's gnashing jaws.

abilities.name: Water Bubble
abilities.desc: Lowers the power of Fire-type moves that hit the Pokémon and prevents it from being burned.

abilities.name: Water Absorb
abilities.desc: If hit by a Water-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

zukanDescs.desc: When bathed in sunlight, this Pokémon emits a pleasantly sweet scent, which causes bug Pokémon to gather around it.
During the day, Fomantis basks in sunlight and sleeps peacefully. It wakes and moves around at night.
Fomantis hates having its naps interrupted. It fires off beams using energy it gathers by bathing in the sun.
Many Trainers give their Fomantis their own flowerpots so they can sunbathe in peace and quiet.

abilities.name: Leaf Guard
abilities.desc: Prevents status conditions in harsh sunlight.

abilities.name: Contrary
abilities.desc: Reverses any stat changes affecting the Pokémon so that attempts to boost its stats instead lower them— and attempts to lower its stats will boost them.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon resembles a beautiful flower. A properly raised Lurantis will have gorgeous, brilliant colors.
The petals on this Pokémon's arms are thin and super sharp, and they can fire laser beams if Lurantis gathers light first.
By masquerading as a bug Pokémon, it lowers the guard of actual bug Pokémon lured in by a scent of sweet flowers. Its sickles bring them down.
A lot of time and effort is required to maintain the vivid colors of its petals. This Pokémon puts its Trainer's attentiveness to the test.

abilities.name: Leaf Guard
abilities.desc: Prevents status conditions in harsh sunlight.

abilities.name: Contrary
abilities.desc: Reverses any stat changes affecting the Pokémon so that attempts to boost its stats instead lower them— and attempts to lower its stats will boost them.

zukanDescs.desc: Pokémon living in the forest eat the delicious caps on Morelull's head. The caps regrow overnight.
Morelull live in forests that stay dark even during the day. They scatter flickering spores that put enemies to sleep.

abilities.name: Illuminate
abilities.desc: By illuminating its surroundings, the Pokémon prevents its accuracy from being lowered.

abilities.name: Effect Spore
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may inflict poison, sleep, or paralysis on the attacker.

abilities.name: Rain Dish
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gradually regains HP in rain.

zukanDescs.desc: Its flickering spores lure in prey and put them to sleep. Once this Pokémon has its prey snoozing, it drains their vitality with its fingertips.
If you see a light deep in a forest at night, don't go near. Shiinotic will make you fall fast asleep.

abilities.name: Illuminate
abilities.desc: By illuminating its surroundings, the Pokémon prevents its accuracy from being lowered.

abilities.name: Effect Spore
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may inflict poison, sleep, or paralysis on the attacker.

abilities.name: Rain Dish
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gradually regains HP in rain.

zukanDescs.desc: Its venom sacs produce a fluid that this Pokémon then heats up with the flame in its tail. This process creates Salandit's poisonous gas.
This sneaky Pokémon will slink behind its prey and immobilize it with poisonous gas before the prey even realizes Salandit is there.
It taunts its prey and lures them into narrow, rocky areas where it then sprays them with toxic gas to make them dizzy and take them down.
Only female Salandit can produce gas laden with pheromones. Males entranced by this gas will do whatever the females tell them.

abilities.name: Corrosion
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can poison the target even if it's a Steel or Poison type.

abilities.name: Oblivious
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is oblivious, keeping it from being infatuated, falling for taunts, or being affected by Intimidate.

zukanDescs.desc: Only female Salazzle exist. They emit a gas laden with pheromones to captivate male Salandit.
The winner of competitions between Salazzle is decided by which one has the most male Salandit with it.
Salazzle makes its opponents light-headed with poisonous gas, then captivates them with alluring movements to turn them into loyal servants.
When two Salazzle meet, they will use their pheromone gas to fight over the males in each other's group.

abilities.name: Corrosion
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can poison the target even if it's a Steel or Poison type.

abilities.name: Oblivious
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is oblivious, keeping it from being infatuated, falling for taunts, or being affected by Intimidate.

zukanDescs.desc: Its fluffy fur is a delight to pet, but carelessly reaching out to touch this Pokémon could result in painful retaliation.
The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.

abilities.name: Fluffy
abilities.desc: Halves the damage taken from moves that make direct contact, but doubles that of Fire-type moves.

abilities.name: Klutz
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can't use any held items.

abilities.name: Cute Charm
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may infatuate attackers that make direct contact with it.

zukanDescs.desc: Once it accepts you as a friend, it tries to show its affection with a hug. Letting it do that is dangerous—it could easily shatter your bones.
The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.

abilities.name: Fluffy
abilities.desc: Halves the damage taken from moves that make direct contact, but doubles that of Fire-type moves.

abilities.name: Klutz
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can't use any held items.

abilities.name: Unnerve
abilities.desc: Unnerves opposing Pokémon and makes them unable to eat Berries.

zukanDescs.desc: Its body gives off a sweet, fruity scent that is extremely appetizing to bird Pokémon.
When under attack, it secretes a sweet and delicious sweat. The scent only calls more enemies to it.
Its sweat is sweet, like syrup made from boiled- down fruit. Because of this, Bounsweet was highly valued in the past, when sweeteners were scarce.
A Greedent drawn in by the sweet scent of these Pokémon will tuck the Bounsweet in among the berries in its tail and carry them all away.

abilities.name: Leaf Guard
abilities.desc: Prevents status conditions in harsh sunlight.

abilities.name: Oblivious
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is oblivious, keeping it from being infatuated, falling for taunts, or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Sweet Veil
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: As it twirls like a dancer, a sweet smell spreads out around it. Anyone who inhales the scent will feel a surge of happiness.
Any Corvisquire that pecks at this Pokémon will be greeted with a smack from its sepals followed by a sharp kick.
Steenee spreads a sweet scent that makes others feel invigorated. This same scent is popular for antiperspirants.
Steenee bounces energetically through forests. If the rind that peels off its body is pulverized, it can be used to treat stomach pains.

abilities.name: Leaf Guard
abilities.desc: Prevents status conditions in harsh sunlight.

abilities.name: Oblivious
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is oblivious, keeping it from being infatuated, falling for taunts, or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Sweet Veil
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: This feared Pokémon has long, slender legs and a cruel heart. It shows no mercy as it stomps on its opponents.
A kick from the hardened tips of this Pokémon's legs leaves a wound in the opponent's body and soul that will never heal.
This Pokémon is proud and aggressive. However, it is said that a Tsareena will instantly become calm if someone touches the crown on its calyx.
This Pokémon launches fierce yet elegant kicks with its long, slender legs. It views Quaquaval as its rival.

abilities.name: Leaf Guard
abilities.desc: Prevents status conditions in harsh sunlight.

abilities.name: Queenly Majesty
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's majesty pressures opponents and makes them unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.

abilities.name: Sweet Veil
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon and its allies from falling asleep.

zukanDescs.desc: Comfey picks flowers with its vine and decorates itself with them. For some reason, flowers won't wither once they're attached to a Comfey.
These Pokémon smell very nice. All Comfey wear different flowers, so each of these Pokémon has its own individual scent.
It uses its vine to pick flowers. The flowers that Comfey attaches to itself develop healing properties.
It prefers areas with lots of flowers. A Comfey adorned with flowers is a happy Comfey, and the flowers gain healing effects.

abilities.name: Flower Veil
abilities.desc: Ally Grass-type Pokémon are protected from status conditions and the lowering of their stats.

abilities.name: Triage
abilities.desc: Gives priority to the Pokémon's healing moves.

abilities.name: Natural Cure
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's status conditions are cured when it switches out.

zukanDescs.desc: With waves of its fan—made from leaves and its own fur—Oranguru skillfully gives instructions to other Pokémon.
It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.
People used to mistake Oranguru for a human when they saw it issue command after command to the other Pokémon of the forest.
This Pokémon lives quietly in the depths of the forest. The purple, cape-like fur gets longer and longer as Oranguru ages.

abilities.name: Inner Focus
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's intense focus prevents it from flinching or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Telepathy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon anticipates and dodges the attacks of its allies.

abilities.name: Symbiosis
abilities.desc: The Pokémon passes its held item to an ally that has used up an item.

zukanDescs.desc: Displaying amazing teamwork, they follow the orders of their boss as they all help out in the search for their favorite berries.
Passimian live in groups of about 20, with each member performing an assigned role. Through cooperation, the group survives.
This Pokémon battles by throwing hard berries. It won't obey a Trainer who throws Poké Balls without skill.
The boss chooses 10 members of the group to go out hunting. The hunting party will evenly split the food they find with the rest of the group.

abilities.name: Receiver
abilities.desc: The Pokémon copies the Ability of a defeated ally.

abilities.name: Defiant
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon has any stat lowered by an opposing Pokémon, its Attack stat will be boosted sharply.

zukanDescs.desc: It's nature's cleaner—it eats anything and everything, including garbage and rotten things. The ground near its nest is always clean.
Wimpod gather in swarms, constantly on the lookout for danger. They scatter the moment they detect an enemy's presence.
Its habitat includes everything from beaches to seabeds. It is happy to eat up even rotten scraps, providing a sort of natural cleaning service.

abilities.name: Wimp Out
abilities.desc: The Pokémon cowardly switches out when its HP drops to half or less.

zukanDescs.desc: It will do anything to win, taking advantage of every opening and finishing opponents off with the small claws on its front legs.
They live in sunken ships or in holes in the seabed. When Golisopod and Grapploct battle, the loser becomes the winner's meal.
It has the skill to cleave even air or seawater in two with a flash of its giant, sharp claws.

abilities.name: Emergency Exit
abilities.desc: The Pokémon, sensing danger, switches out when its HP drops to half or less.

zukanDescs.desc: It uses four of its arms to fiercely assail its foes. Once they've been pushed to the brink of defeat, it finishes them off with the arms it kept hidden.
Grudges of the dead have possessed a mound of sand and become a Pokémon. Sandygast is fond of the shovel on its head.
Sandygast mainly inhabits beaches. It takes control of anyone who puts their hand into its mouth, forcing them to make its body bigger.
If it loses its shovel, it will stick something else— like a branch—in its head to make do until it finds another shovel.
It flings sand into the eyes of its prey and tries to close in on them while they're blinded, but since Sandygast is so slow, the prey tends to escape.
Grudges of the dead have possessed a mound of sand and become a Pokémon. Sandygast is fond of the shovel on its head.

abilities.name: Water Compaction
abilities.desc: Boosts the Defense stat sharply when the Pokémon is hit by a Water-type move.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

zukanDescs.desc: Palossand is known as the Beach Nightmare. It pulls its prey down into the sand by controlling the sand itself, and then it sucks out their souls.
This Pokémon lives on beaches, but it hates water. Palossand can't maintain its castle-like shape if it gets drenched by a heavy rain.
The terrifying Palossand drags smaller Pokémon into its sandy body. Once its victims are trapped, it drains them of their vitality whenever it pleases.
From the hollows in its arms, it fires the bones of its victims, which are all dried up after being drained of their vitality.
Heaps of bones lie buried beneath its castle. They came from those whom Palossand drained of vitality and left all dried up.

abilities.name: Water Compaction
abilities.desc: Boosts the Defense stat sharply when the Pokémon is hit by a Water-type move.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

zukanDescs.desc: It lives in warm, shallow waters. If it encounters a foe, it will spit out its internal organs as a means to punch them.
It's covered in a slime that keeps its skin moist, allowing it to stay on land for days without drying up.

abilities.name: Innards Out
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon is knocked out, it damages its attacker by the amount equal to the HP it had left before it was hit.

abilities.name: Unaware
abilities.desc: When attacking, the Pokémon ignores the target's stat changes.

zukanDescs.desc: Rumor has it that the theft of top-secret research notes led to a new instance of this Pokémon being created in the Galar region.
It was modeled after a mighty Pokémon of myth. The mask placed upon it limits its power in order to keep it under control.

abilities.name: Battle Armor
abilities.desc: Hard armor protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

zukanDescs.desc: A solid bond of trust between this Pokémon and its Trainer awakened the strength hidden within Silvally. It can change its type at will.
The final factor needed to release this Pokémon's true power was a strong bond with a Trainer it trusts.

abilities.name: RKS System
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the memory disc it holds.

zukanDescs.desc: Thanks to its awakened powers, it can change to the Fighting type to match the memory it has equipped.
It has the ability to change its type based on the memory it's holding. This is its Fighting-type form.

abilities.name: RKS System
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the memory disc it holds.

zukanDescs.desc: Thanks to its awakened powers, it can change to the Flying type to match the memory it has equipped.
It has the ability to change its type based on the memory it's holding. This is its Flying-type form.

abilities.name: RKS System
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the memory disc it holds.

zukanDescs.desc: Thanks to its awakened powers, it can change to the Poison type to match the memory it has equipped.
It has the ability to change its type based on the memory it's holding. This is its Poison-type form.

abilities.name: RKS System
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the memory disc it holds.

zukanDescs.desc: Thanks to its awakened powers, it can change to the Ground type to match the memory it has equipped.
It has the ability to change its type based on the memory it's holding. This is its Ground-type form.

abilities.name: RKS System
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the memory disc it holds.

zukanDescs.desc: Thanks to its awakened powers, it can change to the Rock type to match the memory it has equipped.
It has the ability to change its type based on the memory it's holding. This is its Rock-type form.

abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the memory disc it holds.

abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the memory disc it holds.

zukanDescs.desc: Thanks to its awakened powers, it can change to the Fairy type to match the memory it has equipped.
It has the ability to change its type based on the memory it's holding. This is its Fairy-type form.

abilities.name: RKS System
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to match the memory disc it holds.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon is born in the ozone layer. It falls to the ground once the outer shell enclosing its core grows heavy.
It will react when touched or called to, but it's unknown whether such responses indicate consciousness.

abilities.name: Shields Down
abilities.desc: When its HP drops to half or less, the Pokémon's shell breaks and it becomes aggressive.

zukanDescs.desc: Minior that fall to the ground try to return to space, but their wish will not come true—all they can do is fade away.
It feeds on dust in the atmosphere. The color of its core is said to be determined by the composition of the dust it eats.

abilities.name: Shields Down
abilities.desc: When its HP drops to half or less, the Pokémon's shell breaks and it becomes aggressive.

abilities.name: Shields Down
abilities.desc: When its HP drops to half or less, the Pokémon's shell breaks and it becomes aggressive.

zukanDescs.desc: Komala spends its entire life sleeping. It feeds on leaves that contain a potent poison only Komala can break down.
A potent anesthetic can be made by diluting Komala's drool. This anesthetic was used for surgeries in the past.

abilities.name: Comatose
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is always drowsing and will never wake up. It can attack while in its sleeping state.

zukanDescs.desc: Explosive substances coat the shell on its back. Enemies that dare attack it will be blown away by an immense detonation.
Eating sulfur in its volcanic habitat is what causes explosive compounds to develop in its shell. Its droppings are also dangerously explosive.

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.desc: A hard shell protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

zukanDescs.desc: With the long hairs on its back, this Pokémon takes in electricity from other electric Pokémon. It stores what it absorbs in an electric sac.
When it's in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

abilities.name: Iron Barbs
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's iron barbs damage the attacker if it makes direct contact.

abilities.name: Lightning Rod
abilities.desc: The Pokémon draws in all Electric-type moves. Instead of taking damage from them, its Sp. Atk stat is boosted.

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon cannot be knocked out by a single hit as long as its HP is full. One-hit KO moves will also fail to knock it out.

zukanDescs.desc: It wears a rag fashioned into a Pikachu costume in an effort to look less scary. Unfortunately, the costume only makes it creepier.
There was a scientist who peeked under Mimikyu's old rag in the name of research. The scientist died of a mysterious disease.
This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.
Mimikyu was only recently identified as a Pokémon. Previously, people thought it was just a ghost wearing a cloth.
A lonely Pokémon, it conceals its terrifying appearance beneath an old rag so it can get closer to people and other Pokémon.

abilities.name: Disguise
abilities.desc: Once per battle, the shroud that covers the Pokémon can protect it from an attack.

zukanDescs.desc: There will be no forgiveness for any who reveal that it was pretending to be Pikachu. It will bring the culprit down, even at the cost of its own life.
Its disguise made from an old rag allowed it to avoid an attack, but the impact broke the neck of the disguise. Now everyone knows it's a Mimikyu.
Even as it struggles beneath the cloth to repair the broken neck of its disguise, Mimikyu keeps up its impersonation of Pikachu.
It has taken damage and can no longer hold the head of its cloth disguise upright. For as long as it lives, Mimikyu will never forget its attacker.
Mimikyu is sad that it may be exposed for what it really is. It will take revenge on whoever broke the neck of its disguise—no forgiveness.

abilities.name: Disguise
abilities.desc: Once per battle, the shroud that covers the Pokémon can protect it from an attack.

zukanDescs.desc: It grinds its teeth with great force to stimulate its brain. It fires the psychic energy created by this process from the protuberance on its head.
When sunlight reflects on the ripples created by a Bruxish grinding its teeth, the water all around sparkles brilliantly.

abilities.name: Dazzling
abilities.desc: The Pokémon dazzles its opponents, making them unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.

abilities.name: Strong Jaw
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's strong jaw boosts the power of its biting moves.

abilities.name: Wonder Skin
abilities.desc: Makes status moves more likely to miss the Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: The mountains it calls home are nearly two miles in height. On rare occasions, it descends to play with the children living in the towns below.
Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it's angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.
It has a compassionate personality, but if it is angered, it completely destroys its surroundings with its intense breath.

abilities.name: Berserk
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat when it takes a hit that causes its HP to drop to half or less.

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Grass-type moves. Instead, its Attack stat is boosted.

abilities.name: Cloud Nine
abilities.desc: Eliminates the effects of weather.

zukanDescs.desc: Drampa's cells have been invigorated, allowing it to regain its youth. It manipulates the atmosphere to summon storms.
After a piece of seaweed merged with debris from a sunken ship, it was reborn as this ghost Pokémon.
After lowering its anchor, it waits for its prey. It catches large Wailord and drains their life-force.

abilities.name: Steelworker
abilities.desc: Powers up Steel-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: They learn to fight by smashing their head scales together. The dueling strengthens both their skills and their spirits.
Jangmo-o strikes its scales to communicate with others of its kind. Its scales are actually fur that's become as hard as metal.
The scale atop its head is useful for both offense and defense. It bravely stands and faces its foes rather than turning tail, even if its foes are strong.
They communicate with their allies using the sounds their scales make when struck. A group of these Pokémon causes quite a racket.

abilities.name: Bulletproof
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from ball and bomb moves.

abilities.name: Soundproof
abilities.desc: Soundproofing gives the Pokémon full immunity to all sound-based moves.

abilities.name: Overcoat
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage from sandstorms. It is also protected from the effects of powders and spores.

zukanDescs.desc: The scaleless, scarred parts of its body are signs of its strength. It shows them off to defeated opponents.
Before attacking its enemies, it clashes its scales together and roars. Its sharp claws shred the opposition.
It gets into fistfights so intense that the scales on its arms peel off. Bare arms are a sign of valiance.
It fires itself up by striking its scales with force as it dances. Its roar is a battle cry.

abilities.name: Bulletproof
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from ball and bomb moves.

abilities.name: Soundproof
abilities.desc: Soundproofing gives the Pokémon full immunity to all sound-based moves.

abilities.name: Overcoat
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage from sandstorms. It is also protected from the effects of powders and spores.

zukanDescs.desc: It clatters its tail scales to unnerve opponents. This Pokémon will battle only those who stand steadfast in the face of this display.
Certain ruins have paintings of ancient warriors wearing armor made of Kommo-o scales.
It lets its arm hang, and then—quick as a flash— swings upward to land its specialty punch and send the opponent flying high into the sky.
It bashes its scales to test its opponents' mettle. The sound of struck Kommo-o scales frightens weaker foes and sends them running.

abilities.name: Bulletproof
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from ball and bomb moves.

abilities.name: Soundproof
abilities.desc: Soundproofing gives the Pokémon full immunity to all sound-based moves.

abilities.name: Overcoat
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage from sandstorms. It is also protected from the effects of powders and spores.

abilities.desc: Turns the ground into Electric Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.

abilities.desc: The Pokémon anticipates and dodges the attacks of its allies.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon came from another universe. Its gaseous body is so light that even a gentle breeze can blow it away.
Cosmog is very curious but not very cautious, often placing itself in danger. If things start to look dicey, it teleports away.

abilities.name: Unaware
abilities.desc: When attacking, the Pokémon ignores the target's stat changes.

zukanDescs.desc: The absorption of starlight fuels this Pokémon's growth. The shell that encases it is harder than any known material.
It sucks in dust from the air at an astounding rate, frantically building up energy within its core as preparation for evolution.

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon cannot be knocked out by a single hit as long as its HP is full. One-hit KO moves will also fail to knock it out.

zukanDescs.desc: When light radiates from its body, this Pokémon could almost appear to be the sun. It will dispel any darkness and light up the world.
Solgaleo was once known as the Beast That Devours the Sun. Energy in the form of light radiates boundlessly from it.

abilities.name: Full Metal Body
abilities.desc: Prevents other Pokémon's moves or Abilities from lowering the Pokémon's stats.

zukanDescs.desc: Known as the Beast That Calls the Moon, this Pokémon lives by taking in any and all light and converting it into its own energy.
It steals the light from its surroundings and then becomes the full moon, showering its own light across the night sky.

abilities.name: Shadow Shield
abilities.desc: Reduces the amount of damage the Pokémon takes while its HP is full.

abilities.name: Beast Boost
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat every time it knocks out a target.

abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat every time it knocks out a target.

zukanDescs.desc: It survives by absorbing light. After a long time spent slumbering underground, impurities accumulated within it, causing its body to darken.
It needs light to survive, and it goes on a rampage seeking it out. Its laser beams will cut anything to pieces.

abilities.name: Prism Armor
abilities.desc: Reduces the power of supereffective attacks that hit the Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: When it dominates Solgaleo, it takes on this form. It's a vicious Pokémon, mangling prey with its many claws—including those on its back.
Necrozma has attached itself to Solgaleo. It siphons away its host's limitless energy, exploiting that energy to fuel a rampage.

abilities.name: Prism Armor
abilities.desc: Reduces the power of supereffective attacks that hit the Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: Necrozma has subjugated Lunala entirely, forcing the unfortunate Pokémon to emit its light energy for Necrozma to consume.
When Necrozma latches on to Lunala, it becomes vicious, seeing enemies everywhere it looks. It will burn the world with lasers.

abilities.name: Prism Armor
abilities.desc: Reduces the power of supereffective attacks that hit the Pokémon.

abilities.name: Neuroforce
abilities.desc: Powers up the Pokémon's supereffective attacks even further.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon was built by a scientist roughly 500 years ago. The part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.

abilities.name: Soul-Heart
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat every time another Pokémon faints.

zukanDescs.desc: This Magearna looks as it did at the time of its invention. Its magnificent ornamentation delighted the king's daughter.

abilities.name: Soul-Heart
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat every time another Pokémon faints.

zukanDescs.desc: This is Magearna once a previously hidden mode activates. The emotions Magearna had begun to feel now hide away as it fells foe after foe.
A mechanism to remove Magearna's limitations has lain secretly within Magearna for 500 years. This mechanism is triggered by a Mega Stone.
By slipping into the shadow of a martial arts master and copying their movements, this Pokémon learned the ultimate techniques.
This Pokémon can conceal itself in any shadow, so it went undiscovered for a long time.
It slips into others' shadows and mimics their movements and powers. With time, its prowess will surpass that of those it originally imitated.

abilities.name: Technician
abilities.desc: Powers up weak moves so the Pokémon can deal more damage with them.

abilities.name: Beast Boost
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat every time it knocks out a target.

zukanDescs.desc: It runs as fast as lightning strikes, shredding its opponents with its high-voltage claws.
Electricity sparks from the pads on its limbs. Wherever Zeraora runs, lightning flashes and thunder echoes.
Electricity sparks from the pads on its limbs. Wherever Zeraora runs, lightning flashes and thunder echoes.

abilities.name: Volt Absorb
abilities.desc: If hit by an Electric-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

zukanDescs.desc: It stores up 10 lightning strikes' worth of electricity. When it stops limiting itself, it's in the strongest class of electric Pokémon.
It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.
It melts particles of iron and other metals found in the subsoil, so it can absorb them into its body of molten steel.
It dissolves and eats metal. Circulating liquid metal within its body is how it generates energy.
They live as a group, but when the time comes, one strong Meltan will absorb all the others and evolve.
Its body is made up of molten steel. It melts particles of iron and other metals it finds in the ground and absorbs them into itself.

abilities.name: Magnet Pull
abilities.desc: Prevents Steel-type Pokémon from fleeing by pulling them in with magnetism.

zukanDescs.desc: Revered long ago for its capacity to create iron from nothing, for some reason it has come back to life after 3,000 years.
Revered long ago for its capacity to create iron from nothing, for some reason it has come back to life after 3,000 years.
At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.
Centrifugal force is behind the punches of Melmetal's heavy hex-nut arms. Melmetal is said to deliver the strongest punches of all Pokémon.
It was worshipped long ago for its capacity to produce iron. For some reason, it has come back to life after 3,000 years.

abilities.name: Iron Fist
abilities.desc: Powers up punching moves.

zukanDescs.desc: At the end of its life-span, Melmetal will rust and fall apart. The small shards left behind will eventually be reborn as Meltan.
Centrifugal force is behind the punches of Melmetal's heavy hex-nut arms. Melmetal is said to deliver the strongest punches of all Pokémon.

abilities.name: Iron Fist
abilities.desc: Powers up punching moves.

zukanDescs.desc: When it uses its special stick to strike up a beat, the sound waves produced carry revitalizing energy to the plants and flowers in the area.
It attacks with rapid beats of its stick. As it strikes with amazing speed, it gets more and more pumped.
The stick Grookey holds has grown harder and more flexible after soaking in the energy that emanates from inside Grookey's body.
As Grookey strikes everything in sight with its stick, it has more and more fun, and its rhythm becomes livelier.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Grassy Surge
abilities.desc: Turns the ground into Grassy Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.

zukanDescs.desc: The faster a Thwackey can beat out a rhythm with its two sticks, the more respect it wins from its peers.
When it's drumming out rapid beats in battle, it gets so caught up in the rhythm that it won't even notice that it's already knocked out its opponent.
It skillfully uses its two sticks not only for drumming out rapid beats but also for pummeling opponents during battle.
It becomes intensely focused while it drums out vigorous beats—so much so that sometimes it won't listen to its Trainer's commands.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Grassy Surge
abilities.desc: Turns the ground into Grassy Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.

zukanDescs.desc: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.
The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.
Though it has a calm disposition, it won't tolerate those who drum up discord. It strictly disciplines offenders until they learn their lesson.
When one Rillaboom challenges another who leads a troop, a drumming battle commences. The victor becomes the troop's new boss.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Grassy Surge
abilities.desc: Turns the ground into Grassy Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.

zukanDescs.desc: By drumming, it taps into the power of its special tree stump. The roots of the stump follow its direction in battle.
The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.

abilities.name: Overgrow
abilities.desc: Powers up Grass-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Grassy Surge
abilities.desc: Turns the ground into Grassy Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle.

zukanDescs.desc: A warm-up of running around gets fire energy coursing through this Pokémon's body. Once that happens, it's ready to fight at full power.
It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it's raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.
Once its body has heated up, Scorbunny can use the full extent of its power. That's why it does warm-up exercises.
Fire energy gathers in the pads of its feet, raising their temperature. Once hot, Scorbunny's footpads can deal heavy damage to opponents.

abilities.name: Blaze
abilities.desc: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Libero
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once each time the Pokémon enters battle.

zukanDescs.desc: Its thick and fluffy fur protects it from the cold and enables it to use hotter fire moves.
It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.
Thanks to its soft and fluffy fur, Raboot can easily heat up its fire energy and produce flames with more power.
While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves, it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its flames have heated up its forehead.

abilities.name: Blaze
abilities.desc: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Libero
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once each time the Pokémon enters battle.

zukanDescs.desc: It juggles a pebble with its feet, turning it into a burning soccer ball. Its shots strike opponents hard and leave them scorched.
It's skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.
Although Cinderace loses its cool easily, it will battle flawlessly for a Trainer it trusts.
Using a ball that it made out of flames, Cinderace blinds its opponents and keeps them at bay.

abilities.name: Blaze
abilities.desc: Powers up Fire-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Libero
abilities.desc: Changes the Pokémon's type to the type of the move it's about to use. This works only once each time the Pokémon enters battle.

It's skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

zukanDescs.desc: When scared, this Pokémon cries. Its tears pack the chemical punch of 100 onions, and attackers won't be able to resist weeping.
When it gets wet, its skin changes color, and this Pokémon becomes invisible as if it were camouflaged.
It gets berries to eat by shooting them down with bullets of water it spurts from its mouth. Its aim is perfect.
It's a very cautious Pokémon. When it has no choice but to battle, it hides itself before attacking.

abilities.name: Torrent
abilities.desc: Powers up Water-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Sniper
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon's attack lands a critical hit, the attack is powered up even further.

zukanDescs.desc: A clever combatant, this Pokémon battles using water balloons created with moisture secreted from its palms.
Highly intelligent but also very lazy, it keeps enemies out of its territory by laying traps everywhere.
It extends its long, slimy tongue at blinding speeds and finishes off its prey with great skill.
Highly intelligent but also very lazy, it defends its home by laying traps.

abilities.name: Torrent
abilities.desc: Powers up Water-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Sniper
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon's attack lands a critical hit, the attack is powered up even further.

zukanDescs.desc: It has many hidden capabilities, such as fingertips that can shoot water and a membrane on its back that it can use to glide through the air.
Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes' weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.
It uses special lenses in its eyes to sense things about its opponents—like their body heat—then attacks once it identifies a weak spot.
It may present itself as being well-mannered, but deep down, it still has a lazy side. It will slack off when its Trainer isn't looking.

abilities.name: Torrent
abilities.desc: Powers up Water-type moves when the Pokémon's HP is low.

abilities.name: Sniper

zukanDescs.desc: Found throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon becomes uneasy if its cheeks are ever completely empty of berries.
It eats berries nonstop—a habit that has made it more resilient than it looks. It'll show up on farms, searching for yet more berries.
It stores berries in its cheeks. When there are no berries to be found, Skwovet will stuff pebbles into its cheeks to stave off its cravings.
No matter how much it stuffs its belly with food, it is always anxious about getting hungry again. So, it stashes berries in its cheeks and tail.

abilities.name: Cheek Pouch
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's HP is restored when it eats any Berry, in addition to the Berry's usual effect.

abilities.name: Gluttony
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon is holding a Berry to be eaten when its HP is low, it will instead eat the Berry when its HP drops to half or less.

zukanDescs.desc: It stashes berries in its tail—so many berries that they fall out constantly. But this Pokémon is a bit slow-witted, so it doesn't notice the loss.
Common throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon has strong teeth and can chew through the toughest of berry shells.
If it spots a berry tree, it will immediately go to gather berries without a sideways glance—even if it's in the middle of a battle.
This Pokémon makes off with heaps of fallen berries by wrapping them in its tail, which is roughly twice the length of its body.

abilities.name: Cheek Pouch

abilities.name: Gluttony

zukanDescs.desc: It will bravely challenge any opponent, no matter how powerful. This Pokémon benefits from every battle—even a defeat increases its strength a bit.
Jumping nimbly about, this small-bodied Pokémon takes advantage of even the slightest opportunity to disorient larger opponents.
This Pokémon is brave and reckless. The white markings around a Rookidee's eyes intimidate fainthearted Pokémon.
The females are fussier than the males. If another creature dirties a female Rookidee's wings, it'll peck the offender relentlessly in a burning rage.
This valiant Pokémon will challenge any foe, no matter how mighty. Even if that backfires and Rookidee loses, it grows from the experience.

abilities.name: Keen Eye
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's keen eyes prevent its accuracy from being lowered.

abilities.name: Unnerve
abilities.desc: Unnerves opposing Pokémon and makes them unable to eat Berries.

abilities.name: Big Pecks
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon from having its Defense stat lowered.

zukanDescs.desc: Smart enough to use tools in battle, these Pokémon have been seen picking up rocks and flinging them or using ropes to wrap up enemies.
The lessons of many harsh battles have taught it how to accurately judge an opponent's strength.
It's said that the reason behind Corvisquire's high level of intelligence is the large size of its brain relative to those of other bird Pokémon.
This intelligent Pokémon will quickly learn how to use any tool it can hold in its beak or its talons.
Corvisquire are intelligent enough to use tools. They'll use their feet to pick up and fling pebbles or wrap their enemies in ropes.

abilities.name: Keen Eye

abilities.desc: Unnerves opposing Pokémon and makes them unable to eat Berries.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon reigns supreme in the skies of the Galar region. The black luster of its steel body could drive terror into the heart of any foe.
With their great intellect and flying skills, these Pokémon very successfully act as the Galar region's airborne taxi service.
Corviknight can't serve as a taxi service in Paldea because the Pokémon's natural predators will attack it while it flies, endangering the customer.
Although its wings have partly turned to steel and become heavy as a result, this Pokémon flies through the skies with ease.
Its wings, now made of steel, are extremely tough. Even if it's attacked mid-flight, Corviknight flies on mightily through the sky, unbothered.

abilities.name: Pressure
abilities.desc: Puts other Pokémon under pressure, causing them to expend more PP to use their moves.

abilities.name: Unnerve
abilities.desc: Unnerves opposing Pokémon and makes them unable to eat Berries.

abilities.desc: Bounces back only the stat-lowering effects that the Pokémon receives.

As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

zukanDescs.desc: Aided by the soft pads on its feet, it silently raids the food stores of other Pokémon. It survives off its ill-gotten gains.
Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.
It filches food from people and Pokémon alike. While fleeing the scene, it wipes its tracks away with its tail to throw off pursuers.

With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.
This clever Pokémon marks potential targets with its scent, then follows its nose to locate their nests.

abilities.name: Stakeout
abilities.desc: Doubles the damage dealt to a target that has just switched into battle.

zukanDescs.desc: It anchors itself in the ground with its single leg, then basks in the sun. After absorbing enough sunlight, its petals spread as it blooms brilliantly.
It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Cotton Down
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by an attack, it scatters cotton fluff around and lowers the Speed stats of all Pokémon except itself.

abilities.name: Regenerator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon has a little of its HP restored when withdrawn from battle.

abilities.name: Effect Spore
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may inflict poison, sleep, or paralysis on the attacker.

zukanDescs.desc: The seeds attached to its cotton fluff are full of nutrients. It spreads them on the wind so that plants and other Pokémon can benefit from them.
The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

abilities.name: Cotton Down
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon is hit by an attack, it scatters cotton fluff around and lowers the Speed stats of all Pokémon except itself.

abilities.name: Regenerator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon has a little of its HP restored when withdrawn from battle.

abilities.name: Effect Spore
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may inflict poison, sleep, or paralysis on the attacker.

zukanDescs.desc: Its curly fleece is such an effective cushion that this Pokémon could fall off a cliff and stand right back up at the bottom, unharmed.
If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won't be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

abilities.name: Fluffy
abilities.desc: Halves the damage taken from moves that make direct contact, but doubles that of Fire-type moves.

abilities.name: Run Away
abilities.desc: Enables a sure getaway from wild Pokémon.

abilities.name: Bulletproof
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from ball and bomb moves.

zukanDescs.desc: Weave a carpet from its springy wool, and you end up with something closer to a trampoline. You'll start to bounce the moment you set foot on it.
Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

abilities.name: Fluffy
abilities.desc: Halves the damage taken from moves that make direct contact, but doubles that of Fire-type moves.

abilities.name: Steadfast
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's determination boosts its Speed stat every time it flinches.

abilities.name: Bulletproof
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from ball and bomb moves.

zukanDescs.desc: Apparently the itch of its teething impels it to snap its jaws at anything in front of it.
It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.
Its large front tooth is still growing in. When the tooth itches, this Pokémon will bite another Chewtle's horn, and the two Pokémon will tussle.
A popular game among children is to lift up sticks that Chewtle have bitten onto to see whose Chewtle can hang on the longest.

abilities.name: Strong Jaw
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's strong jaw boosts the power of its biting moves.

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.desc: A hard shell protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

abilities.name: Swift Swim
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in rain.

zukanDescs.desc: With jaws that can shear through steel rods, this highly aggressive Pokémon chomps down on its unfortunate prey.
This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.
Its massive, jagged teeth can crush a boulder in a single bite. This Pokémon has an extremely vicious disposition.
Drednaw lurks along the shoreline. When prey come to drink water, Drednaw stretches its neck out and chomps down on them.

abilities.name: Strong Jaw
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's strong jaw boosts the power of its biting moves.

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.desc: A hard shell protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

abilities.name: Swift Swim
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in rain.

zukanDescs.desc: With jaws that can shear through steel rods, this highly aggressive Pokémon chomps down on its unfortunate prey.
This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

abilities.name: Strong Jaw
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's strong jaw boosts the power of its biting moves.

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.desc: A hard shell protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

abilities.name: Swift Swim
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in rain.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon is very popular as a herding dog in the Galar region. As it runs, it generates electricity from the base of its tail.
This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

abilities.name: Ball Fetch
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon is not holding an item, it will fetch the Poké Ball from the first failed throw of the battle.

abilities.name: Rattled
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gets scared when hit by a Dark-, Ghost-, or Bug-type attack or if intimidated, which boosts its Speed stat.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon generates electricity and channels it into its legs to keep them going strong. Boltund can run nonstop for three full days.
It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

abilities.name: Strong Jaw
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's strong jaw boosts the power of its biting moves.

abilities.name: Competitive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Sp. Atk stat sharply when its stats are lowered by an opposing Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: Most of its body has the same composition as coal. Fittingly, this Pokémon was first discovered in coal mines about 400 years ago.
It can race around like a unicycle, even on rough, rocky terrain. Burning coal sustains it.
On sunny days, Rolycoly will come out onto grassy plains and roll around. Then it will eat the grass that gets tangled in its wheel-like leg.
Chunks of the surface of this Pokémon's body that have grown old and flaked off have long been used for fuel as an alternative to coal.

abilities.name: Steam Engine
abilities.desc: Boosts the Speed stat drastically when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire- or Water-type move.

abilities.name: Heatproof
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's heatproof body halves the damage taken from Fire-type moves.

abilities.name: Flash Fire
abilities.desc: If hit by a Fire-type move, the Pokémon absorbs the flames and uses them to power up its own Fire-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It forms coal inside its body. Coal dropped by this Pokémon once helped fuel the lives of people in the Galar region.
By rapidly rolling its legs, it can travel at over 18 mph. The temperature of the flames it breathes exceeds 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.
The temperature inside its body increases when it experiences strong emotions. It rolls around frantically while spewing flames.
Due to the coal tar created inside it, the heap of coal on Carkol's back never falls apart, even when the Pokémon rolls around at high speeds.

abilities.name: Steam Engine
abilities.desc: Boosts the Speed stat drastically when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire- or Water-type move.

abilities.name: Flame Body
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

abilities.name: Flash Fire
abilities.desc: If hit by a Fire-type move, the Pokémon absorbs the flames and uses them to power up its own Fire-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It's usually peaceful, but the vandalism of mines enrages it. Offenders will be incinerated with flames that reach 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit.
While it's engaged in battle, its mountain of coal will burn bright red, sending off sparks that scorch the surrounding area.
To intimidate its opponents, Coalossal will vigorously shake its body, scattering coal from its smoldering back.
It's gentle usually but fearsome when angered. With a body that burns at over 2,700 degrees Fahrenheit, it crushes foes and turns them to ash.

abilities.name: Steam Engine
abilities.desc: Boosts the Speed stat drastically when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire- or Water-type move.

abilities.name: Flame Body
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

abilities.name: Flash Fire
abilities.desc: If hit by a Fire-type move, the Pokémon absorbs the flames and uses them to power up its own Fire-type moves.

While it's engaged in battle, its mountain of coal will burn bright red, sending off sparks that scorch the surrounding area.

abilities.name: Flash Fire
abilities.desc: If hit by a Fire-type move, the Pokémon absorbs the flames and uses them to power up its own Fire-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It spends its entire life inside an apple. It hides from its natural enemies, bird Pokémon, by pretending it's just an apple and nothing more.
As soon as it's born, it burrows into an apple. Not only does the apple serve as its food source, but the flavor of the fruit determines its evolution.
It grows by eating the flesh of an apple. Applin uses its own body fluid to strengthen the apple's skin and prevent it from rotting.
Applin lives inside an apple. When an Applin is separated from its apple, its body loses moisture and the Pokémon grows weak.

abilities.name: Ripen
abilities.desc: Ripens Berries and doubles their effect.

abilities.name: Gluttony
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon is holding a Berry to be eaten when its HP is low, it will instead eat the Berry when its HP drops to half or less.

abilities.name: Bulletproof
abilities.desc: Protects the Pokémon from ball and bomb moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.
It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.
It flutters around while seeking an opening in the opponent's guard, then attacks by spitting acidic liquid strong enough to melt metal.
It uses its own body fluid to repair its apple. Strong Flapple that have won many battles have apples that are clay colored all over.

abilities.name: Ripen
abilities.desc: Ripens Berries and doubles their effect.

abilities.name: Gluttony
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon is holding a Berry to be eaten when its HP is low, it will instead eat the Berry when its HP drops to half or less.

abilities.name: Hustle
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat but lowers its accuracy.

zukanDescs.desc: It ate a sour apple, and that induced its evolution. In its cheeks, it stores an acid capable of causing chemical burns.
It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.

abilities.name: Ripen
abilities.desc: Ripens Berries and doubles their effect.

abilities.name: Gluttony
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon is holding a Berry to be eaten when its HP is low, it will instead eat the Berry when its HP drops to half or less.

abilities.name: Hustle

zukanDescs.desc: Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.
Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.
It feeds mainly on sweet apples. It will also eat small bug Pokémon that are attracted by its sweet nectar.
When some Lechonk come along to lick the sweet nectar on Appletun's back, Appletun will spew sticky nectar at them to drive them away.

abilities.name: Ripen
abilities.desc: Ripens Berries and doubles their effect.

abilities.name: Gluttony
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon is holding a Berry to be eaten when its HP is low, it will instead eat the Berry when its HP drops to half or less.

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is protected by a layer of thick fat, which halves the damage taken from Fire- and Ice-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: Eating a sweet apple caused its evolution. A nectarous scent wafts from its body, luring in the bug Pokémon it preys on.
Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.

abilities.name: Gluttony

zukanDescs.desc: As it digs, it swallows sand and stores it in its neck pouch. The pouch can hold more than 17 pounds of sand.
It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.
Silicobra's neck pouch, which can inflate and deflate like a balloon, gets more elastic each time Silicobra sheds its skin.
Silicobra's large nostrils are specialized for spraying sand, so this Pokémon is not very good at telling apart different smells.

abilities.name: Sand Spit
abilities.desc: The Pokémon creates a sandstorm when it's hit by an attack.

abilities.name: Shed Skin
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may cure its own status conditions by shedding its skin.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

zukanDescs.desc: When it contracts its body, over 220 pounds of sand sprays from its nose. If it ever runs out of sand, it becomes disheartened.
Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.
It will expand its body as much as it can and then contract itself, blasting out sand with enough force to wash away a dump truck.
The sand it spews contains sharp, pointy gravel, which is actually just sand that Sandaconda's saliva has hardened into irregular shapes.

abilities.name: Sand Spit
abilities.desc: The Pokémon creates a sandstorm when it's hit by an attack.

abilities.name: Shed Skin
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may cure its own status conditions by shedding its skin.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

zukanDescs.desc: When it contracts its body, over 220 pounds of sand sprays from its nose. If it ever runs out of sand, it becomes disheartened.
Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.

abilities.name: Sand Spit
abilities.desc: The Pokémon creates a sandstorm when it's hit by an attack.

abilities.name: Shed Skin
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may cure its own status conditions by shedding its skin.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

zukanDescs.desc: It's so strong that it can knock out some opponents in a single hit, but it also may forget what it's battling midfight.
This hungry Pokémon swallows Arrokuda whole. Occasionally, it makes a mistake and tries to swallow a Pokémon other than its preferred prey.
Cramorant instinctively swallow everything whole. They're at their most formidable when they're struggling to swallow overly large prey.
It's an incredibly poor learner because it uses the bare minimum amount of energy for its brain. It focuses on battling instead.

abilities.name: Gulp Missile
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon uses Surf or Dive, it will come back with prey. When it takes damage, it will spit out the prey to attack.

zukanDescs.desc: Cramorant's gluttony led it to try to swallow an Arrokuda whole, which in turn led to Cramorant getting an Arrokuda stuck in its throat.
The slightest shock is sometimes enough to get this Pokémon to spit out the Arrokuda lodged in its throat.
Cramorant is constantly on the hunt for food along riverbanks. It loves Arrokuda—when it finds them, it swallows them whole without a thought.
Cramorant takes advantage of Arrokuda's frantic thrashing to help spit Arrokuda out at high speed.

abilities.name: Gulp Missile
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon uses Surf or Dive, it will come back with prey. When it takes damage, it will spit out the prey to attack.

zukanDescs.desc: This Cramorant has accidentally gotten a Pikachu lodged in its gullet. Cramorant is choking a little, but it isn't really bothered.
The half-swallowed Pikachu is so startled that it isn't struggling yet, but it's still looking for a chance to strike back.
Its throat is clearly not wide enough to swallow a Pikachu, and yet Cramorant makes no move to release its catch.
With a Pikachu stuck in its throat, Cramorant can use Pikachu's prickling electricity to give its opponents a shock.

abilities.name: Gulp Missile
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon uses Surf or Dive, it will come back with prey. When it takes damage, it will spit out the prey to attack.

zukanDescs.desc: If it sees any movement around it, this Pokémon charges for it straightaway, leading with its sharply pointed jaw. It's very proud of that jaw.
After it's eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That's when Cramorant swallows it up.
Arrokuda can swim quickly only in a straight line. The ones that become sluggish from overeating are the first to be targeted by flocks of Wattrel.
It takes down prey by charging into them with its hard, pointed jaw. But Arrokuda's eyesight is poor, so this tactic has a low success rate.

abilities.name: Swift Swim
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in rain.

abilities.name: Propeller Tail
abilities.desc: Ignores the effects of opposing Pokémon's Abilities and moves that draw in moves.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon has a jaw that's as sharp as a spear and as strong as steel. Apparently Barraskewda's flesh is surprisingly tasty, too.
It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.
It spins its tail fins to leap from the water, then viciously bites down on Wingull flying close to the water's surface.
It swims at speeds of over 100 knots and battles fiercely with pods of Finizen over prey.

abilities.name: Swift Swim
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in rain.

abilities.name: Propeller Tail
abilities.desc: Ignores the effects of opposing Pokémon's Abilities and moves that draw in moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It stores poison in an internal poison sac and secretes that poison through its skin. If you touch this Pokémon, a tingling sensation follows.
It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.
It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel's body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.
This selfish, attention-seeking Pokémon stores poison and electricity in two different sacs inside its body.
It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The wattage is weak, but it can cause a tingling numbness.

abilities.name: Rattled
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gets scared when hit by a Dark-, Ghost-, or Bug-type attack or if intimidated, which boosts its Speed stat.

abilities.name: Static
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is charged with static electricity and may paralyze attackers that make direct contact with it.

abilities.name: Klutz
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can't use any held items.

zukanDescs.desc: When this Pokémon sounds as if it's strumming a guitar, it's actually clawing at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity.
This short-tempered and aggressive Pokémon chugs stagnant water to absorb any toxins it might contain.
As it scatters toxic sweat and emits electricity, a melody that sounds like it came from a guitar reverberates through the surrounding area.
The jolts of electricity it launches by violently strumming the protrusions on its chest easily exceed 15,000 volts.
When Toxtricity claws at the protrusions on its chest to generate electricity, a sound like that of a guitar echoes through the air.

abilities.name: Punk Rock
abilities.desc: Boosts the power of sound-based moves. The Pokémon also takes half the damage from these kinds of moves.

abilities.name: Plus
abilities.desc: Boosts the Sp. Atk stat of the Pokémon if an ally with the Plus or Minus Ability is also in battle.

abilities.name: Technician
abilities.desc: Powers up weak moves so the Pokémon can deal more damage with them.

This short-tempered and aggressive Pokémon chugs stagnant water to absorb any toxins it might contain.

abilities.name: Technician

zukanDescs.desc: Capable of generating 15,000 volts of electricity, this Pokémon looks down on all that would challenge it.
It has an electrical organ on its chest. While generating electricity, it fills its surroundings with what sounds like the strumming of a bass guitar.
Many youths admire the way this Pokémon listlessly picks fights and keeps its cool no matter what opponent it faces.
As it gulps down stagnant water and generates electricity in its body, a sound like a rhythm played by a bass guitar reverberates all around.
On its chest is an electricity-generating organ. A sound like that of a bass guitar echoes all around while this organ produces electricity.

abilities.name: Punk Rock
abilities.desc: Boosts the power of sound-based moves. The Pokémon also takes half the damage from these kinds of moves.

abilities.name: Minus
abilities.desc: Boosts the Sp. Atk stat of the Pokémon if an ally with the Plus or Minus Ability is also in battle.

abilities.desc: Powers up weak moves so the Pokémon can deal more damage with them.

zukanDescs.desc: It stores flammable gas in its body and uses it to generate heat. The yellow sections on its belly get particularly hot.
It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they're well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

abilities.name: Flash Fire
abilities.desc: If hit by a Fire-type move, the Pokémon absorbs the flames and uses them to power up its own Fire-type moves.

abilities.name: White Smoke
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is protected by its white smoke, which prevents other Pokémon from lowering its stats.

abilities.name: Flame Body
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

zukanDescs.desc: When it heats up, its body temperature reaches about 1,500 degrees Fahrenheit. It lashes its body like a whip and launches itself at enemies.
While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

abilities.name: Flash Fire
abilities.desc: If hit by a Fire-type move, the Pokémon absorbs the flames and uses them to power up its own Fire-type moves.

abilities.name: White Smoke
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is protected by its white smoke, which prevents other Pokémon from lowering its stats.

abilities.name: Flame Body
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may burn the attacker.

While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.
