abilities.name: Anger Shell
abilities.desc: When an attack causes its HP to drop to half or less, the Pokémon gets angry. This lowers its Defense and Sp. Def stats but boosts its Attack, Sp. Atk, and Speed stats.

abilities.name: Shell Armor
abilities.desc: A hard shell protects the Pokémon from critical hits.

abilities.name: Regenerator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon has a little of its HP restored when withdrawn from battle.

zukanDescs.desc: The more sunlight this Pokémon bathes in, the more spicy chemicals are produced by its body, and thus the spicier its moves become.
Traditional Paldean dishes can be extremely spicy because they include the shed front teeth of Capsakid among their ingredients.
Capsakid is prone to intense mood swings, perhaps influenced by the spicy chemicals inside it. It will bite anything it takes a disliking to.

abilities.name: Chlorophyll
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in harsh sunlight.

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's insomnia prevents it from falling asleep.

abilities.name: Klutz
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can't use any held items.

zukanDescs.desc: The red head converts spicy chemicals into fire energy and blasts the surrounding area with a super spicy stream of flame.
The green head has turned vicious due to the spicy chemicals stimulating its brain. Once it goes on a rampage, there is no stopping it.
Its two heads have different temperaments and excel at different fighting styles. Nevertheless, they act in perfect harmony on the battlefield.

abilities.name: Chlorophyll
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in harsh sunlight.

abilities.name: Insomnia
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's insomnia prevents it from falling asleep.

abilities.name: Moody
abilities.desc: Every turn, one of the Pokémon's stats will be boosted sharply but another stat will be lowered.

zukanDescs.desc: Mega Evolution has dialed up this Pokémon's spiciness. It swings its "necktie" around to wallop its foes.
This Pokémon creates a mud ball by mixing sand and dirt with psychic energy. It treasures its mud ball more than its own life.
It rolls its mud ball around while the energy it needs for evolution matures. Eventually the time comes for it to evolve.

abilities.name: Compound Eyes
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's compound eyes boost its accuracy.

abilities.name: Shed Skin
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may cure its own status conditions by shedding its skin.

zukanDescs.desc: The body that supports the ball barely moves. Therefore, it is thought that the true body of this Pokémon is actually inside the ball.
An infant sleeps inside the ball. Rabsca rolls the ball soothingly with its legs to ensure the infant sleeps comfortably.

abilities.name: Synchronize
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon is burned, paralyzed, or poisoned by another Pokémon, that Pokémon will be inflicted with the same status condition.

abilities.name: Telepathy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon anticipates and dodges the attacks of its allies.

zukanDescs.desc: Flittle's toes levitate about half an inch above the ground because of the psychic power emitted from the frills on the Pokémon's belly.
It spends its time running around wastelands. If anyone steals its beloved berries, it will chase them down and exact its revenge.

abilities.name: Anticipation
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can sense an opposing Pokémon's dangerous moves.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

abilities.name: Speed Boost
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's Speed stat is boosted every turn.

zukanDescs.desc: It immobilizes opponents by bathing them in psychic power from its large eyes. Despite its appearance, it has a vicious temperament.
It emits psychic power from the gaps between its multicolored frills and sprints at speeds greater than 120 mph.

abilities.name: Opportunist
abilities.desc: If an opponent's stat is boosted, the Pokémon seizes the opportunity to boost the same stat for itself.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

abilities.name: Speed Boost
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's Speed stat is boosted every turn.

zukanDescs.desc: It swings its handmade hammer around to protect itself, but the hammer is often stolen by Pokémon that eat metal.
This Pokémon pounds iron scraps together to make a hammer. It will remake the hammer again and again until it's satisfied with the result.
Tinkatink cobbles together a suitable hammer for itself using tools and metal fragments it finds in mines.

abilities.name: Mold Breaker
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

abilities.name: Own Tempo
abilities.desc: The Pokémon sticks to its own tempo, preventing it from becoming confused or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Pickpocket
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from attackers that make direct contact with it.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon will attack groups of Pawniard and Bisharp, gathering metal from them in order to create a large and sturdy hammer.
These Pokémon make their homes in piles of scrap metal. They test the strength of each other's hammers by smashing them together.
Tinkatuff sometimes come to human settlements, where they rummage through factories and warehouses to gather metal.

abilities.name: Mold Breaker
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

abilities.name: Own Tempo
abilities.desc: The Pokémon sticks to its own tempo, preventing it from becoming confused or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Pickpocket
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from attackers that make direct contact with it.

zukanDescs.desc: This intelligent Pokémon has a very daring disposition. It knocks rocks into the sky with its hammer, aiming for flying Corviknight.
The hammer tops 220 pounds, yet it gets swung around easily by Tinkaton as it steals whatever it pleases and carries its plunder back home.
A Tinkaton leader teaches the Tinkatink in its group the skills needed for pounding and shaping metal.

abilities.name: Mold Breaker
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

abilities.name: Own Tempo
abilities.desc: The Pokémon sticks to its own tempo, preventing it from becoming confused or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Pickpocket
abilities.desc: The Pokémon steals the held item from attackers that make direct contact with it.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon can pick up the scent of a Veluza just over 65 feet away and will hide itself in the sand.
Though it looks like Diglett, Wiglett is an entirely different species. The resemblance seems to be a coincidental result of environmental adaptation.

abilities.name: Gooey
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

abilities.name: Rattled
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gets scared when hit by a Dark-, Ghost-, or Bug-type attack or if intimidated, which boosts its Speed stat.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

zukanDescs.desc: It has a vicious temperament, contrary to what its appearance may suggest. It wraps its long bodies around prey, then drags the prey into its den.
A variety of fish Pokémon, Wugtrio was once considered to be a regional form of Dugtrio.

abilities.name: Gooey
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon lowers the attacker's Speed stat.

abilities.name: Rattled
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gets scared when hit by a Dark-, Ghost-, or Bug-type attack or if intimidated, which boosts its Speed stat.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

zukanDescs.desc: It gathers things up in an apron made from shed feathers added to the Pokémon's chest feathers, then drops those things from high places for fun.
Bombirdier uses the apron on its chest to bundle up food, which it carries back to its nest. It enjoys dropping things that make loud noises.

abilities.name: Big Pecks
abilities.desc: Prevents the Pokémon from having its Defense stat lowered.

abilities.name: Keen Eye
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's keen eyes prevent its accuracy from being lowered.

abilities.name: Rocky Payload
abilities.desc: Powers up Rock-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It likes playing with others of its kind using the water ring on its tail. It uses ultrasonic waves to sense the emotions of other living creatures.
Its water ring is made from seawater mixed with a sticky fluid that Finizen secretes from its blowhole.

abilities.name: Water Veil
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's water veil prevents it from being burned.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon changes its appearance if it hears its allies calling for help. Palafin will never show anybody its moment of transformation.
Its physical capabilities are no different than a Finizen's, but when its allies are in danger, it transforms and powers itself up.

abilities.name: Zero to Hero
abilities.desc: The Pokémon transforms into its Hero Form when it switches out.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon's ancient genes have awakened. It is now so extraordinarily strong that it can easily lift a cruise ship with one fin.
This hero of the ocean swims at a speed of 50 knots and saves drowning people and Pokémon.

abilities.name: Zero to Hero
abilities.desc: The Pokémon transforms into its Hero Form when it switches out.

zukanDescs.desc: It is said that this Pokémon was born when an unknown poison Pokémon entered and inspirited an engine left at a scrap-processing factory.
The steel section is Varoom's actual body. This Pokémon clings to rocks and converts the minerals within into energy to fuel its activities.

abilities.name: Overcoat
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage from sandstorms. It is also protected from the effects of powders and spores.

abilities.name: Slow Start
abilities.desc: For five turns, the Pokémon's Attack and Speed stats are halved.

zukanDescs.desc: It creates a gas out of poison and minerals from rocks. It then detonates the gas in its cylinders— now numbering eight—to generate energy.
Revavroom viciously threatens others with the sound of its exhaust. It sticks its tongue out from its cylindrical mouth and sprays toxic fluids.

abilities.name: Overcoat
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage from sandstorms. It is also protected from the effects of powders and spores.

abilities.name: Filter
abilities.desc: Reduces the power of supereffective attacks that hit the Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: Apparently Cyclizar has been allowing people to ride on its back since ancient times. Depictions of this have been found in 10,000-year-old murals.
It can sprint at over 70 mph while carrying a human. The rider's body heat warms Cyclizar's back and lifts the Pokémon's spirit.
Cyclizar keeps energy stored in the folds of its throat, allowing it to run nonstop over very long distances while carrying someone.

abilities.name: Shed Skin
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may cure its own status conditions by shedding its skin.

abilities.name: Regenerator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon has a little of its HP restored when withdrawn from battle.

zukanDescs.desc: When attacked, this Pokémon will wield the tendrils on its body like fists and pelt the opponent with a storm of punches.
This Pokémon lives in arid deserts. It maintains its metal body by consuming iron from the soil.

abilities.name: Earth Eater
abilities.desc: If hit by a Ground-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

abilities.name: Sand Veil
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in a sandstorm.

zukanDescs.desc: It absorbs nutrients from cave walls. The petals it wears are made of crystallized poison.
Glimmet's toxic mineral crystals look just like flower petals. This Pokémon scatters poisonous powder like pollen to protect itself.
Glimmet clings to the ceilings of caves. It defends itself by striking enemies with its bud, which is packed with poisonous components.

abilities.name: Toxic Debris
abilities.desc: Scatters poison spikes at the feet of the opposing team when the Pokémon takes damage from physical moves.

abilities.name: Corrosion
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can poison the target even if it's a Steel or Poison type.

zukanDescs.desc: When this Pokémon detects danger, it will open up its crystalline petals and fire beams from its conical body.
Glimmora's petals are made of crystallized poison energy. It has recently become evident that these petals resemble Tera Jewels.
Glimmora blooms into a large flower made of poisonous components. It works together with groups of Glimmet to repel enemies.

abilities.name: Toxic Debris
abilities.desc: Scatters poison spikes at the feet of the opposing team when the Pokémon takes damage from physical moves.

abilities.name: Corrosion
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can poison the target even if it's a Steel or Poison type.

zukanDescs.desc: Glimmora's petals—now larger and separated from its main body—rotate around it to provide defense while scattering poisonous fragments.
It is said that a dog Pokémon that died in the wild without ever interacting with a human was reborn as this Pokémon.
This friendly Pokémon doesn't like being alone. Pay it even the slightest bit of attention, and it will follow you forever.
This Pokémon loves to gambol and roughhouse. It rubs its fur up against others and absorbs their life force until they're utterly drained.

abilities.name: Pickup
abilities.desc: The Pokémon may pick up an item another Pokémon used during a battle. It may pick up items outside of battle, too.

abilities.name: Fluffy
abilities.desc: Halves the damage taken from moves that make direct contact, but doubles that of Fire-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: Houndstone spends most of its time sleeping in graveyards. Among all the dog Pokémon, this one is most loyal to its master.
A lovingly mourned Pokémon was reborn as Houndstone. It doesn't like anyone touching the protuberance atop its head.
At some point during the many years it spent guarding the gravestone of its dearly departed master, it was reborn as a ghost Pokémon.

abilities.name: Sand Rush
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in a sandstorm.

abilities.name: Fluffy
abilities.desc: Halves the damage taken from moves that make direct contact, but doubles that of Fire-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon apparently ties the base of its neck into a knot so that energy stored in its belly does not escape from its beak.
Thanks to a behavior of theirs known as "synchronizing," an entire flock of these Pokémon can attack simultaneously in perfect harmony.
A flock of Flamigo can reach numbers of over 10,000. The sight of one of these massive flocks moving together in sync is incredible to behold.

abilities.name: Scrappy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon can hit Ghost-type Pokémon with Normal- and Fighting-type moves. It is also unaffected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Tangled Feet
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness if it is confused.

abilities.name: Costar
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it copies an ally's stat changes.

zukanDescs.desc: This species left the ocean and began living on land a very long time ago. It seems to be closely related to Wailmer.
It lives in frigid regions in pods of five or so individuals. It loves the minerals found in snow and ice.

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is protected by a layer of thick fat, which halves the damage taken from Fire- and Ice-type moves.

abilities.name: Snow Cloak
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's evasiveness in snow.

abilities.name: Sheer Force
abilities.desc: Removes any additional effects from the Pokémon's moves, but increases the moves' power.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon wanders around snowy, icy areas. It protects its body with powerful muscles and a thick layer of fat under its skin.
Ice energy builds up in the horn on its upper jaw, causing the horn to reach cryogenic temperatures that freeze its surroundings.

abilities.name: Thick Fat
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is protected by a layer of thick fat, which halves the damage taken from Fire- and Ice-type moves.

abilities.name: Slush Rush
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Speed stat in snow.

abilities.name: Sheer Force
abilities.desc: Removes any additional effects from the Pokémon's moves, but increases the moves' power.

zukanDescs.desc: When Veluza discards unnecessary flesh, its mind becomes honed and its psychic power increases. The spare flesh has a mild but delicious flavor.
Veluza has excellent regenerative capabilities. It sheds spare flesh from its body to boost its agility, then charges at its prey.

abilities.name: Mold Breaker
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

abilities.name: Sharpness
abilities.desc: Powers up slicing moves.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon is a glutton, but it's bad at getting food. It teams up with a Tatsugiri to catch prey.
It treats Tatsugiri like its boss and follows it loyally. Though powerful, Dondozo is apparently not very smart.
Although it boasts fantastic strength and a huge body that stirs up great waves when it thrashes, Dondozo struggles at catching prey.

abilities.name: Unaware
abilities.desc: When attacking, the Pokémon ignores the target's stat changes.

abilities.name: Oblivious
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is oblivious, keeping it from being infatuated, falling for taunts, or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Water Veil
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's water veil prevents it from being burned.

zukanDescs.desc: This is a small dragon Pokémon. It lives inside the mouth of Dondozo to protect itself from enemies on the outside.
Tatsugiri is an extremely cunning Pokémon. It feigns weakness to lure in prey, then orders its partner to attack.
Tatsugiri will battle furiously among themselves to determine which of them will get to partner with a particular Dondozo.

abilities.name: Commander
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it goes inside the mouth of an ally Dondozo if one is on the field. The Pokémon then issues commands from there.

abilities.name: Storm Drain
abilities.desc: The Pokémon draws in all Water-type moves. Instead of taking damage from them, its Sp. Atk stat is boosted.

zukanDescs.desc: This species' differing colors and patterns are apparently the result of Tatsugiri changing itself to suit the preferences of the prey it lures in.
This Pokémon tricks its opponents by playing dead. It is small and weak, but it uses its smarts to survive.
Even if swallowed by a foe, Tatsugiri will try to make its escape through various means, such as by inflating its throat sac.

abilities.name: Commander
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it goes inside the mouth of an ally Dondozo if one is on the field. The Pokémon then issues commands from there.

abilities.name: Storm Drain
abilities.desc: The Pokémon draws in all Water-type moves. Instead of taking damage from them, its Sp. Atk stat is boosted.

zukanDescs.desc: Bird Pokémon are its main prey. This Pokémon knows it's weak, so it hunts with a partner.
It's one of the most intelligent dragon Pokémon. It camouflages itself by inflating its throat sac.
Tatsugiri assesses situations using its sharp intellect. If it determines there is no hope of winning, it flees as fast as it can.

abilities.name: Commander
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it goes inside the mouth of an ally Dondozo if one is on the field. The Pokémon then issues commands from there.

abilities.name: Storm Drain
abilities.desc: The Pokémon draws in all Water-type moves. Instead of taking damage from them, its Sp. Atk stat is boosted.

zukanDescs.desc: Tatsugiri's brain has been invigorated by Mega Evolution, making it even wilier. It can create and command copies of itself.
It solidifies the energy of Mega Evolution, building up an overflowing pile to launch as projectiles. These projectiles explode on contact.
Using the energy of Mega Evolution, it creates a dish to ride upon, allowing it to move with total freedom—even through the air.
When its anger rose beyond a critical point, this Pokémon gained power that is unfettered by the limits of its physical body.
It imbues its fists with the power of the rage that it kept hidden in its heart. Opponents struck by these imbued fists will be shattered to their core.
Primeape evolved into Annihilape after repeatedly using a move with enough force to burst the blood vessels in its head.

abilities.name: Vital Spirit
abilities.desc: The Pokémon is full of vitality, and that prevents it from falling asleep.

abilities.name: Inner Focus
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's intense focus prevents it from flinching or being affected by Intimidate.

abilities.name: Defiant
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon has any stat lowered by an opposing Pokémon, its Attack stat will be boosted sharply.

zukanDescs.desc: When attacked, this Pokémon will retaliate by sticking thick spines out from its body. It's a risky move that puts everything on the line.
It lives at the bottom of ponds and swamps. It will carry Wooper on its back and ferry them across water from one shore to the other.

abilities.name: Poison Point
abilities.desc: Contact with the Pokémon may poison the attacker.

abilities.name: Water Absorb
abilities.desc: If hit by a Water-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

abilities.name: Unaware
abilities.desc: When attacking, the Pokémon ignores the target's stat changes.

zukanDescs.desc: Now that the brain waves from the head and tail are synced up, the psychic power of this Pokémon is 10 times stronger than Girafarig's.
The hardened head from the tail protects the head of the main body as Farigiraf whips its long neck around to headbutt enemies.

abilities.name: Cud Chew
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon eats a Berry, it will regurgitate that Berry at the end of the next turn and eat it one more time.

abilities.name: Armor Tail
abilities.desc: The mysterious tail covering the Pokémon's head makes opponents unable to use priority moves against the Pokémon or its allies.

abilities.name: Sap Sipper
abilities.desc: The Pokémon takes no damage when hit by Grass-type moves. Instead, its Attack stat is boosted.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon uses its hard tail to make its nest by boring holes into bedrock deep underground. The nest can reach lengths of over six miles.
It drives enemies out of its nest by sucking in enough air to fill its long, narrow lungs, then releasing the air in an intense blast.

abilities.name: Serene Grace
abilities.desc: Raises the likelihood of additional effects occurring when the Pokémon uses its moves.

abilities.name: Run Away
abilities.desc: Enables a sure getaway from wild Pokémon.

abilities.name: Rattled
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gets scared when hit by a Dark-, Ghost-, or Bug-type attack or if intimidated, which boosts its Speed stat.

zukanDescs.desc: The gentle Dudunsparce will put Pokémon that wander into its nest onto its back and carry them to the entrance.
A recent study uncovered that the number of segments a Dudunsparce's body has is determined by the Pokémon's genes.

abilities.name: Serene Grace
abilities.desc: Raises the likelihood of additional effects occurring when the Pokémon uses its moves.

abilities.name: Run Away
abilities.desc: Enables a sure getaway from wild Pokémon.

abilities.name: Rattled
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gets scared when hit by a Dark-, Ghost-, or Bug-type attack or if intimidated, which boosts its Speed stat.

zukanDescs.desc: Only a Bisharp that stands above all others in its vast army can evolve into Kingambit.
Though it commands a massive army in battle, it's not skilled at devising complex strategies. It just uses brute strength to keep pushing.

abilities.name: Defiant
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon has any stat lowered by an opposing Pokémon, its Attack stat will be boosted sharply.

abilities.name: Supreme Overlord
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, its Attack and Sp. Atk stats are slightly boosted for each of the allies in its party that have already been defeated.

abilities.name: Pressure
abilities.desc: Puts other Pokémon under pressure, causing them to expend more PP to use their moves.

zukanDescs.desc: Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. The name Great Tusk was taken from a creature listed in a certain book.
This creature resembles a mysterious Pokémon that, according to a paranormal magazine, has lived since ancient times.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: There has been only one reported sighting of this Pokémon. It resembles a mysterious creature depicted in an old expedition journal.
It resembles a mysterious Pokémon described in a paranormal magazine as a Jigglypuff from one billion years ago.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: It is possible that the creature listed as Brute Bonnet in a certain book could actually be this Pokémon.
It bears a slight resemblance to a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a cross between a dinosaur and a mushroom.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon has characteristics similar to those of Flutter Mane, a creature mentioned in a certain book.
It has similar features to a ghostly pterosaur that was covered in a paranormal magazine, but the two have little else in common.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: This mysterious Pokémon has some similarities to a creature that an old book introduced as Slither Wing.
This Pokémon somewhat resembles an ancient form of Volcarona that was introduced in a dubious magazine.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: No records exist of this Pokémon being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon's traits match up with a creature shown in an expedition journal.
It slightly resembles a Magneton that lived for 10,000 years and was featured in an article in a paranormal magazine.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon closely resembles a scientific weapon that a paranormal magazine claimed was sent to this planet by aliens.
Sightings of this Pokémon have occurred in recent years. It resembles a mysterious object described in an old expedition journal.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: Its shape is similar to a robot featured in a paranormal magazine article. The robot was said to have been created by an ancient civilization.
It resembles a mysterious object mentioned in an old book. There are only two reported sightings of this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: It is very similar to a cyborg covered exclusively by a paranormal magazine. The cyborg was said to be the modified form of a certain athlete.
This Pokémon shares many similarities with Iron Hands, an object mentioned in a certain expedition journal.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: It resembles a certain Pokémon introduced in a paranormal magazine, described as the offspring of a Hydreigon that fell in love with a robot.
It's possible that Iron Jugulis, an object described in an old book, may actually be this Pokémon.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon resembles an unknown object described in a paranormal magazine as a UFO sent to observe humanity.
No records exist of this species being caught. Data is lacking, but the Pokémon's traits match up with an object described in an old book.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: It has some similarities to a Pokémon introduced in a dubious magazine as a Tyranitar from one billion years into the future.
Some of its notable features match those of an object named within a certain expedition journal as Iron Thorns.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: Frigibax absorbs heat through its dorsal fin and converts the heat into ice energy. The higher the temperature, the more energy Frigibax stores.
This Pokémon lives in forests and craggy areas. Using the power of its dorsal fin, it cools the inside of its nest like a refrigerator.
Frigibax's dorsal fin can convert heat from fiery moves into Ice-type energy to freeze the air.

abilities.name: Thermal Exchange
abilities.desc: Boosts the Attack stat when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire-type move. The Pokémon also cannot be burned.

abilities.name: Ice Body
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gradually regains HP in snow.

zukanDescs.desc: Arctibax freezes the air around it, protecting its face with an ice mask and turning its dorsal fin into a blade of ice.
It attacks with the blade of its frozen dorsal fin by doing a front flip in the air. Arctibax's strong back and legs allow it to pull off this technique.
Arctibax is armed with a bladed dorsal fin and a mask made of ice. It is agile in its movements, springing upon foes to deliver slashing attacks.

abilities.name: Thermal Exchange
abilities.desc: Boosts the Attack stat when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire-type move. The Pokémon also cannot be burned.

abilities.name: Ice Body
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gradually regains HP in snow.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon blasts cryogenic air out from its mouth. This air can instantly freeze even liquid-hot lava.
It launches itself into battle by flipping upside down and spewing frigid air from its mouth. It finishes opponents off with its dorsal blade.
Baxcalibur exhales air cold enough to freeze lava. It uses its breath to freeze foes, then pulverizes them with a blow from its tail.

abilities.name: Thermal Exchange
abilities.desc: Boosts the Attack stat when the Pokémon is hit by a Fire-type move. The Pokémon also cannot be burned.

abilities.name: Ice Body
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gradually regains HP in snow.

zukanDescs.desc: Baxcalibur's dorsal blade has grown even more massive thanks to Mega Evolution. This Pokémon fires beams from the hilt at its solar plexus.
This Pokémon was born inside a treasure chest about 1,500 years ago. It sucks the life-force out of scoundrels who try to steal the treasure.
It lives inside an old treasure chest. Sometimes it gets left in shop corners since no one realizes it's actually a Pokémon.
It lurks inside a sturdy treasure chest and possesses solid defenses, but it's so heavy that it has difficulty moving around.

abilities.name: Rattled
abilities.desc: The Pokémon gets scared when hit by a Dark-, Ghost-, or Bug-type attack or if intimidated, which boosts its Speed stat.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon was born from passion that seeped into a coin. It wanders, apparently seeking to return to the treasure chest it once inhabited.
It wanders around, carrying an old coin on its back. It survives by draining the life-force from humans who try to pick up its coin.
If it senses it's about to be found by a human or a Pokémon, Gimmighoul will turn invisible to protect itself, leaving only its coin visible.

abilities.name: Run Away
abilities.desc: Enables a sure getaway from wild Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: Its body seems to be made up of 1,000 coins. This Pokémon gets along well with others and is quick to make friends with anybody.
It has a sturdy body made up of stacked coins. Gholdengo overwhelms its enemies by firing coin after coin at them in quick succession.
This Pokémon likes lively places. Some wild Gholdengo even live in cities.

abilities.name: Good as Gold
abilities.desc: A body of pure, solid gold gives the Pokémon full immunity to other Pokémon's status moves.

zukanDescs.desc: The grudge of a person punished for writing the king's evil deeds upon wooden tablets has clad itself in dead leaves to become a Pokémon.
It drains the life-force from vegetation, causing nearby forests to instantly wither and fields to turn barren.

abilities.name: Tablets of Ruin
abilities.desc: The power of the Pokémon's ruinous wooden tablets lowers the Attack stats of all Pokémon except itself.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon can control 100 tons of fallen snow. It plays around innocently by leaping in and out of avalanches it has caused.
The hatred of those who perished by the sword long ago has clad itself in snow and become a Pokémon.

abilities.name: Sword of Ruin
abilities.desc: The power of the Pokémon's ruinous sword lowers the Defense stats of all Pokémon except itself.

zukanDescs.desc: The fear poured into an ancient ritual vessel has clad itself in rocks and dirt to become a Pokémon.
It slowly brings its exceedingly heavy head down upon the ground, splitting the earth open with huge fissures that run over 160 feet deep.

abilities.name: Vessel of Ruin
abilities.desc: The power of the Pokémon's ruinous vessel lowers the Sp. Atk stats of all Pokémon except itself.

zukanDescs.desc: It controls flames burning at over 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit. It casually swims through the sea of lava it creates by melting rock and sand.
The envy accumulated within curved beads that sparked multiple conflicts has clad itself in fire and become a Pokémon.

abilities.name: Beads of Ruin
abilities.desc: The power of the Pokémon's ruinous beads lowers the Sp. Def stats of all Pokémon except itself.

zukanDescs.desc: It is possible that this is the creature listed as Roaring Moon in an expedition journal that still holds many mysteries.
According to an article in a dubious magazine, this Pokémon has some connection to a phenomenon that occurs in a certain region.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: It has some similarities to a mad scientist's invention covered in a paranormal magazine.
It's possible that this is the object listed as Iron Valiant in a certain expedition journal.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: This seems to be the Winged King mentioned in an old expedition journal. It was said to have split the land with its bare fists.
This Pokémon resembles Cyclizar, but it is far burlier and more ferocious. Nothing is known about its ecology or other features.

abilities.name: Orichalcum Pulse
abilities.desc: Turns the sunlight harsh when the Pokémon enters a battle. The ancient pulse thrumming through the Pokémon also boosts its Attack stat in harsh sunlight.

zukanDescs.desc: Much remains unknown about this creature. It resembles Cyclizar, but it is far more ruthless and powerful.
This seems to be the Iron Serpent mentioned in an old book. The Iron Serpent is said to have turned the land to ash with its lightning.

abilities.name: Hadron Engine
abilities.desc: Turns the ground into Electric Terrain when the Pokémon enters a battle. The futuristic engine within the Pokémon also boosts its Sp. Atk stat on Electric Terrain.

zukanDescs.desc: This ferocious creature is shrouded in mystery. It's named after an aquatic monster mentioned in an old expedition journal.
It resembles an illustration published in a paranormal magazine, said to be a depiction of a super-ancient Suicune.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: Many of its physical characteristics match those of a Virizion from the future that was covered in a paranormal magazine.
According to the few eyewitness accounts that exist, it used its shining blades to julienne large trees and boulders.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: Dipplin is two creatures in one Pokémon. Its evolution was triggered by a special apple grown only in one place.
The head sticking out belongs to the fore-wyrm, while the tail belongs to the core-wyrm. The two share one apple and help each other out.

abilities.name: Supersweet Syrup
abilities.desc: A sickly sweet scent spreads across the field the first time the Pokémon enters a battle, lowering the evasiveness of opposing Pokémon.

abilities.name: Gluttony
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon is holding a Berry to be eaten when its HP is low, it will instead eat the Berry when its HP drops to half or less.

abilities.name: Sticky Hold
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's held items cling to its sticky body and cannot be removed by other Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: Supposedly, the regrets of a tea ceremony master who died before perfecting his craft lingered in some matcha and became a Pokémon.
Poltchageist looks like a regional form of Sinistea, but it was recently discovered that the two Pokémon are entirely unrelated.

abilities.name: Hospitality
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it showers its ally with hospitality, restoring a small amount of the ally's HP.

abilities.name: Heatproof
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's heatproof body halves the damage taken from Fire-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It sprinkles some of its powdery body onto food and drains the life-force from those who so much as lick it.
It has taken up residence in a very expensive tea caddy. It takes an expert to distinguish the expensive tea caddies from the cheap ones.

abilities.name: Hospitality
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it showers its ally with hospitality, restoring a small amount of the ally's HP.

abilities.name: Heatproof
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's heatproof body halves the damage taken from Fire-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It pretends to be tea, trying to fool people into drinking it so it can drain their life-force. Its ruse is generally unsuccessful.
It prefers cool, dark places, such as the back of a shelf or the space beneath a home's floorboards. It wanders in search of prey after sunset.

abilities.name: Hospitality
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it showers its ally with hospitality, restoring a small amount of the ally's HP.

abilities.name: Heatproof
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's heatproof body halves the damage taken from Fire-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: It lives inside a superb teacup that was crafted by a potter of great renown. Collectors positively adore this Pokémon.
The more stirring it does with the tea whisk on its head, the more energy it builds up. It does this to prepare for battle.

abilities.name: Hospitality
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it showers its ally with hospitality, restoring a small amount of the ally's HP.

abilities.name: Heatproof
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's heatproof body halves the damage taken from Fire-type moves.

zukanDescs.desc: After all its muscles were stimulated by the toxic chain around its neck, Okidogi transformed and gained a powerful physique.
Okidogi is a ruffian with a short temper. It can pulverize anything by swinging around the chain on its neck.

abilities.name: Toxic Chain
abilities.desc: The power of the Pokémon's toxic chain may badly poison any target the Pokémon hits with a move.

abilities.name: Guard Dog
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's Attack stat if intimidated. Moves and items that would force the Pokémon to switch out also fail to work.

zukanDescs.desc: The chain is made from toxins that enhance capabilities. It stimulated Munkidori's brain and caused the Pokémon's psychic powers to bloom.
Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.

abilities.name: Toxic Chain
abilities.desc: The power of the Pokémon's toxic chain may badly poison any target the Pokémon hits with a move.

abilities.name: Frisk
abilities.desc: When it enters a battle, the Pokémon can check an opposing Pokémon's held item.

zukanDescs.desc: Fezandipiti owes its beautiful looks and lovely voice to the toxic stimulants emanating from the chain wrapped around its body.
Fezandipiti beats its glossy wings to scatter pheromones that captivate people and Pokémon.

abilities.name: Toxic Chain
abilities.desc: The power of the Pokémon's toxic chain may badly poison any target the Pokémon hits with a move.

abilities.name: Technician
abilities.desc: Powers up weak moves so the Pokémon can deal more damage with them.

zukanDescs.desc: This Pokémon's type changes based on which mask it's wearing. It confounds its enemies with nimble movements and kicks.
This mischief-loving Pokémon is full of curiosity. It battles by drawing out the type-based energy contained within its masks.

abilities.name: Defiant
abilities.desc: If the Pokémon has any stat lowered by an opposing Pokémon, its Attack stat will be boosted sharply.

zukanDescs.desc: This form excels in both attack and defense. It ceaselessly unleashes moves like a spring gushes water.
In this form, it draws on the power of water. It attacks unrelentingly with kicks and ivy strikes.

abilities.name: Water Absorb
abilities.desc: If hit by a Water-type move, the Pokémon has its HP restored instead of taking damage.

zukanDescs.desc: This form is the most aggressive, bombarding enemies with the intensity of flames blazing within a hearth.
In this form, it draws on the power of fire. It spears its enemies with thorn-covered ivy.

abilities.name: Mold Breaker
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's moves are unimpeded by the Ability of the target.

zukanDescs.desc: This form has excellent defenses, absorbing impacts solidly like the cornerstones that support houses.
In this form, it draws on the power of stone. Its body is rock-solid, protecting it from all manner of attacks.

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon cannot be knocked out by a single hit as long as its HP is full. One-hit KO moves will also fail to knock it out.

zukanDescs.desc: It gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.
It digs holes on mountains, searching for food. It's so durable that being caught in a cave-in won't faze it.

abilities.name: Stamina
abilities.desc: Boosts the Defense stat when the Pokémon is hit by an attack.

abilities.name: Sturdy
abilities.desc: The Pokémon cannot be knocked out by a single hit as long as its HP is full. One-hit KO moves will also fail to knock it out.

abilities.name: Stalwart
abilities.desc: Ignores the effects of opposing Pokémon's Abilities and moves that draw in moves.

zukanDescs.desc: Seven syrpents live inside an apple made of syrup. The syrpent in the center is the commander.
These capricious syrpents have banded together. On the rare occasion that their moods align, their true power is unleashed.

abilities.name: Supersweet Syrup
abilities.desc: A sickly sweet scent spreads across the field the first time the Pokémon enters a battle, lowering the evasiveness of opposing Pokémon.

abilities.name: Regenerator
abilities.desc: The Pokémon has a little of its HP restored when withdrawn from battle.

abilities.name: Sticky Hold
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's held items cling to its sticky body and cannot be removed by other Pokémon.

zukanDescs.desc: There are scant few reports of this creature being sighted. One short video shows it rampaging and spouting pillars of flame.
It resembles an eerie Pokémon once shown in a paranormal magazine. That Pokémon was said to be an Entei regenerated from a fossil.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: It's said to incinerate everything around it with lightning launched from its fur. Very little is known about this creature.
It bears resemblance to a Pokémon that became a hot topic for a short while after a paranormal magazine touted it as Raikou's ancestor.

abilities.name: Protosynthesis
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat in harsh sunlight or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: It resembles a Pokémon described in a dubious magazine as a Terrakion that had been modified by an evil organization.
It was named after a mysterious object recorded in an old book. Its body seems to be metallic.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: It resembles a mysterious object introduced in a paranormal magazine as a cutting-edge weapon shaped like a Cobalion.
There was supposedly an incident in which it launched shining blades to cut everything around it to pieces. Little else is known about it.

abilities.name: Quark Drive
abilities.desc: Boosts the Pokémon's most proficient stat on Electric Terrain or if the Pokémon is holding Booster Energy.

zukanDescs.desc: Terapagos protects itself using its power to transform energy into hard crystals. This Pokémon is the source of the Terastal phenomenon.
It's thought that this Pokémon lived in ancient Paldea until it got caught in seismic shifts and went extinct.

abilities.name: Tera Shift
abilities.desc: When the Pokémon enters a battle, it absorbs the energy around itself and transforms into its Terastal Form.

zukanDescs.desc: Upon sensing danger, it prepares itself for battle by creating a sturdy shell of crystallized Terastal energy.
The shell is made of crystallized Terastal energy. When struck by a move, this shell absorbs the move's energy and transfers it to Terapagos.

abilities.name: Tera Shell
abilities.desc: The Pokémon's shell contains the powers of each type. All damage-dealing moves that hit the Pokémon when its HP is full will not be very effective.

zukanDescs.desc: In this form, Terapagos resembles the world as the ancients saw it, and its Terastal energy is abnormally amplified.
An old expedition journal describes the sight of this Pokémon buried in the depths of the earth as resembling a planet floating in space.

abilities.name: Teraform Zero
abilities.desc: When Terapagos changes into its Stellar Form, it uses its hidden powers to eliminate all effects of weather and terrain, reducing them to zero.

zukanDescs.desc: It feeds others toxic mochi that draw out desires and capabilities. Those who eat the mochi fall under Pecharunt's control, chained to its will.
Its peach-shaped shell serves as storage for a potent poison. It makes poisonous mochi and serves them to people and Pokémon.

abilities.name: Poison Puppeteer
abilities.desc: Pokémon poisoned by Pecharunt's moves will also become confused.
